## PAGE 02410: (2026x) Automatic Instantiation Wizard

- page_id: `272735077`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735077/2026x+Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:02.999+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This tool allows you to create instances of various entities with just a few clicks. It is useful for working with complex models, and for assembling large systems from parts.

While creating instances automatically, you can:

- Select properties for which the instances will be created.
- Change types of the instantiated properties.
- Change names of the instantiated properties.
- Apply custom stereotypesto the instantiated properties.
- Assign default values for the instantiated properties.

For properties having a multiplicity of more than 1, you can create as many instances of the same type as you need, since parallel parts can be added while creating instances. Previously, this feature had been supported by the **SysML v1 plugin only**.

The **Automatic Instantiation wizard** analyzes the structure of the selected element and collects all available information about that element from the model (attributes, properties, and so on). All this information is presented in the first step of the wizard.

The **Automatic Instantiation wizard** evaluates collected information and provides a suggestion of all possible instances to be created.

If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the **Automatic Instantiation wizard,** you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. But if you want to change default values, create several different configurations, see the **Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard** and the **Case study:** sections on how to do that.

The **Automatic Instantiation Wizard** consists of three steps:

- Step #1. Selecting parts to be instantiated
- Step #2. Selecting a package
- Step #3. Creating a diagram (optional)

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select the elements for which you want to create instances.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

-

  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Link' space='MED']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED']

  - Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study
  - Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML Plugin

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736817 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02411: (2026x) Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study

- page_id: `272736817`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736817/2026x+Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard+Case+Study
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:47.630+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools > (2026x) Automatic Instantiation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Consider this example:**

Let’s say we have three classes named **Administrator**, **Librarian**, and **Personnel**. These classes are connected in the following way:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The multiplicity in the association end near the **Librarian** is 4. In this case, the **Automatic Instantiation wizard** suggests four parts.

In the following figure, these values are displayed as **[1]**, **[2]**, **[3]**, and **[4]**. Each of them have a set of properties inherited from the type of the parts. In this case, we cannot add more parts because the multiplicity points to the particular number of available parts. The type of the part can be changed, if there is a sub element in the model.

In this case, class **Librarian** has a sub-class **Personnel**, so the type of **part [1]** can be changed to **Personnel** as you can see in the following figure.

Let’s select parts and properties for instantiation:

1. Select a new package, wherein our created instance specifications will be stored.
2. Create a new package Instances of the Administrator .
3. Create a new diagram, wherein our created instances will be displayed.
4. After selecting the diagram type (in this case, we will select to create a class diagram) and owner, let’s click the Finish button.
5. See the result in the following figure.

These classes are connected in the following way:</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_classes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p>The multiplicity in the association end near the <strong>Librarian</strong> is 4. The type of the part can be changed, if there is a sub element in the model.</p><p>In this case, class <strong>Librarian</strong> has a sub-class <strong>Personnel</strong>, so the type of <strong>part [1]</strong> can be changed to <strong>Personnel</strong> as you can see in the following figure.</p><p>Let’s select parts and properties for instantiation:</p><ol><li>Select a new package, wherein our created instance specifications will be stored.</li><li>Create a new package <strong>Instances of the Administrator</strong>.</li><li>Create a new diagram, wherein our created instances will be displayed.</li><li>After selecting the diagram type (in this case, we will select to create a class diagram) and owner, let’s click the <strong>Finish</strong> button. </li><li>See the result in the following figure. </li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 02412: (2026x) Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML v1

- page_id: `272731369`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731369/2026x+Automatic+Instantiation+wizard+in+SysML+v1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:48.931+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard analyzes the structure of a selected element and collects all available information about that element from the model (attributes, properties, and so on). It evaluates collected information and suggests all possible instances to be created.

This tool allows you to create instances of various entities with just a few clicks. It is useful for working with complex models, and for assembling large systems from parts. For properties having a multiplicity of more than 1, you can create as many instances of the same type as you need, since parallel parts can be added while creating instances. While creating instances automatically, you can:

- Select properties for which the instances will be created.
- Change types of the instantiated properties.
- Change names of the instantiated properties.
- Apply custom stereotypes to the instantiated properties.
- Assign default values for the instantiated properties.

The wizard consists of the following areas:

- Steps area: displays all steps of the wizard and shows the current step you are working on.
- Element tree: displays the entire internal structure of the selected element.
- *Selection area*: allows you to select the elements to be instantiated.
- Instantiated properties area: allows you to change the default values and create several different sets of instances .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Automatic Instantiation wizard of the *Vehicle* Block with the main areas highlighted: Steps area, Element tree, Selection area, and Instantiated properties area.

##### Using the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

To create and display instance specifications of Blocks in the diagram by using the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select the Block for which you want to create instances.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Instance (see the following figure). [ATTACHMENT filename='create_instance_command.png']
3. Follow the steps of the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='']How to work in each step of the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard >> If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard, you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. To change default values or create several different configurations, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard' space=''].
4. Click Finish when you are done. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] and a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] are created for the selected Block (see the figure below). All part instances are grouped under their owners which allows you to visually identify the structure of nested instances in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Containment tab' space=''] .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Block Definition Diagram with instances of the vehicle Block is created by using the Automatic Instantiation wizard.

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the**VehicleStructure**sample model. To open this sample, download*[ATTACHMENT filename='VehicleStructure.mdzip'].*

- page_id: `272738174`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004562780

1004562782

1004562781

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard is used to automatically instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.
2. Right-click them and choose Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

The wizard consists of these three steps:

truenone

##### Selecting parts

Select elements and their internal parts to instantiate.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting a package

Select, create or clone the package to store the created actual structure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating a diagram

Specify diagram name, select diagram type to visualize the actual structure and the package to store the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1004562777

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Instantiating structures' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736042 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02414: (2026x) Automatic numbering

- page_id: `272736042`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736042/2026x+Automatic+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:33.886+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Generic numbering mechanism > (2026x) Working with generic numbering mechanism
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Make sure that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Numbering Customizations' space=''] are already created.

With an automatic numbering feature, you can number the elements of the selected type upon the element creation or modification. You can specify the number display location after the elements are numbered.

To enable automatic element numbering

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog opens.
2. In the options group list, select or expand the General option and click Numbering either in the options list or in the options group list. The numbering properties opens.
3. Set the Use Element Auto-numbering property value to true .
4. Click OK .

To specify the element number display location

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Symbol Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens.
  - From the main menu, select Options > Project . Then, in the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog , select the Symbol styles > Default group.
2. Select one of the following **Element Numbering Display Mode** property value: Property valueVisualization on the element shapeAbove the element name[IMAGE alt='' src='']Before the element name[IMAGE alt='' src='']Do not display on symbol[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click OK after you have selected.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Manual numbering' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732607 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02415: (2026x) Autosave

- page_id: `272732607`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732607/2026x+Autosave
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:52.107+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After you stop working with the modeling tool,an idle time passes and the current project is saved to a special file called the AutoRecovery file. 
If the application is terminated normally, the AutoRecovery file is removed. If the application crashes, the AutoRecovery file is kept. On startup, the modeling tool checks for an AutoRecovery file. If it exists, the modeling tool suggests loading the project from this file.

To save an AutoRecovery file of the open project(s) when a system is not in use

1. Open the Environment Options dialog.
2. In the General pane, select the Save/Load group, and then set the Save Project Recovery Data on Idle option to true .
3. Enter the system idle time (in minutes) in the Idle Time to Activate Recovery Save box. This is the necessary idle time in order to activate the AutoRecovery save.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After you stop working with the modeling tool,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> an idle time passes</span> and the current project is saved to a special file called the AutoRecovery file. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />If the application is terminated normally, the AutoRecovery file is removed. If the application crashes, the AutoRecovery file is kept. On startup, the modeling tool checks for an AutoRecovery file. If it exists, the modeling tool suggests loading the project from this file.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To save an AutoRecovery file of the open project(s) when a system is not in use</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> pane, select the <strong>Save/Load</strong> group, and then set the <strong>Save Project Recovery Data on Idle </strong>option to <em>true</em>. </li><li>Enter the system idle time (in minutes) in the <strong>Idle</strong> <strong>Time</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>Activate</strong> <strong>Recovery</strong> <strong>Save</strong> box. This is the necessary idle time in order to activate the AutoRecovery save.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731266 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02416: (2026x) Autowire Parts

- page_id: `272731266`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731266/2026x+Autowire+Parts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:40.091+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram
- labels: ['automatic-connections', 'automatic-connectors', 'connect-parts', 'autowire', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Autowire Parts** capability analyzes and finds compatible ports/interfaces, and automatically creates [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED'] between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED']. The figure below demonstrates the *Thermal View* Internal Block diagram before and after Autowire Parts when the entire diagram is selected. This functionality provides a quick and easy way to create multiple connectors simultaneously.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED']and ports are displayed or created in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''], the parts can be related to one another via [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='']Connectors. You can manually create these connections by using the **Connector** button from the part's/port's shape smart manipulator toolbar or from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space='']. You can also do this automatically by using the **Autowire Parts** command from the Tools menu ([IMAGE alt='' src='']) in the diagram toolbar, if the following conditionsconditions are satisfied:

- Parts and ports are displayed on the Internal Block diagram pane.
- The displayed ports are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED'].
- Ports are typed by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED'] that have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED'] defined.
- Types and directions of these flow properties are compatible.
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space=''] do not exist in the model.
- Only one-to-one connection is supported.
- Multiple interfaces are not supported.

See an example [ATTACHMENT filename='Autowire-Parts.mdzip']

The following procedure describes how to use the **Autowire Parts** function. This functionality works for an entire diagram or individually selected parts and ports.

To connect Part Properties via compatible ports automatically

1. Select any number of part or port shapes, or the diagram pane.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Tools > **Autowire Parts**, or press Ctrl+Alt+W. InformationIf new connectors are not created after clicking the **Autowire Parts**, check if all required are satisfied.
3. In the opened Messages window, you can specify two options: [ATTACHMENT filename='autowiring_message.png']
  - Highlight new connections - leave the check box selected, if you want the newly created Connectors to be highlighted on the diagram pane, and be able to confirm or delete them manually.
  - Show this message next time - leave the check box selected, if you want this message to appear the next time you click the Autowire Parts command.
4. Click OK .
5. The Connector(s) between Part Properties via compatible interfaces are created in the model. If you left the Highlight new connection check box selected in the Messages window (step 3, first bullet), you must review the newly created Connectors in the following way (see the figure below): a. Select the Connector. b. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='confirm_connector.png'] and choose: - Confirm Connector - removes the highlight from the Connector. - Delete Connector - removes the Connector from the model. - Ignore - removes the highlight from the Connector. - Select In Validation Results - opens the Active Validation Results table. [ATTACHMENT filename='confirm_connection.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729071 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02418: (2026x) AV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `272729071`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729071/2026x+AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:37.129+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) DoDAF All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-1-overview-and-summary-information', 'av-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229302728

229302730

229302729

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between Architectural Descriptions. The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.

The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:

- In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.
- When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.

The AV-1 may be used to:

- Scope the architecture effort.
- Provide context to the architecture effort.
- Define the architecture effort.
- Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.
- Assist search within an architecture repository.

**Implementation**

The AV-1 can be represented:

- Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='av_1.png']
- Using the following (.docx) reports: - AV-1 Overview and Summary Information. - AV-1 DARS.

The AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.

The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.

229302727

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architectural Description' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architectural Reference' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architecture Metadata' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating document based reports' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating DARS report' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67384731-4fd5-46a6-8a24-1aebd5204ff7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229302728</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd138c28-c458-41d5-8143-35ace235d540"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229302730</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a8b4444-f044-42f5-9635-7440e021c6a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229302729</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between Architectural Descriptions. The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.</p><p>The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:</p><ul><li>In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.</li><li>When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.</li></ul><p>The AV-1 may be used to:</p><ul><li>Scope the architecture effort.</li><li>Provide context to the architecture effort.</li><li>Define the architecture effort.</li><li>Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.</li><li>Assist search within an architecture repository.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The AV-1 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="AV-1 Overview and Summary Information" ac:alt="AV-1 Overview and Summary Information"><ri:attachment ri:filename="av_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) AV-1 Overview and Summary Information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Using the following (.docx) reports:<br /><p>- AV-1 Overview and Summary Information.</p><p>- AV-1 DARS.</p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.</p><p>The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29d928db-eff2-4732-bb69-65fec83182d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229302727</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Architectural Description" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Architectural Reference" /></ac:link></span><br /></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Architecture Metadata" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating DARS report" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating DARS template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729067 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02419: (2026x) AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `272729067`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729067/2026x+AV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:35.922+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) DoDAF All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-2', 'av-2-integrated-dictionary', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229449780

229449782

229449781

**Description**

The AV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).

An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.

It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:

- Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.
- Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.
- Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.
- Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.

**Implementation**

AV-2 can be represented:

- An AV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.

229449779

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Definition' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alias' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Same As' space='MED']

An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).</p><p>An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.</p><p>It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:</p><ul><li>Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.</li><li>Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.</li><li>Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.</li><li>Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>AV-2 can be represented:</p><ul><li>An AV-2 table.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary" ac:alt="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary"><ri:attachment ri:filename="av_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) AV-2 Integrated Dictionary" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736757 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02420: (2026x) Available Transformations

- page_id: `272736757`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736757/2026x+Available+Transformations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:39.011+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools > (2026x) Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Transformations are usually used for converting models between different modeling domains. Transformations are named by the types of their source models and their destination models. These are the available transformations:

- Any to Any . This transformation copies all your model or part of it to another package without making any changes. You can also remap types in the destination model by applying some type mapping rules.
- Profile Migration . Helper transformation for migrating models using one profile to models using another profile (usually - between different versions of the same profile - old and new).

UML to SQL(Generic/Oracle), SQL to UML, UML to XML Schema, XML Schema to UML transformations are available with the separately-installed Cameo Data Modeler plugin (which comes free of charge with Architect and Enterprise Editions and is separately purchasable for Standard and Professional editions).

Additional custom types of transformations can be defined by plugins.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Transformations are usually used for converting models between different modeling domains. Transformations are named by the types of their source models and their destination models. These are the available transformations:</p><ul><li><strong>Any to Any</strong>. This transformation copies all your model or part of it to another package without making any changes. You can also remap types in the destination model by applying some type mapping rules.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Profile Migration</strong>. Helper transformation for migrating models using one profile to models using another profile (usually - between different versions of the same profile - old and new).</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d03b38f1-c9ac-4094-9c27-5adee5b78388"><ac:rich-text-body><p>UML to SQL(Generic/Oracle), SQL to UML, UML to XML Schema, XML Schema to UML transformations are available with the separately-installed Cameo Data Modeler plugin (which comes free of charge with Architect and Enterprise Editions and is separately purchasable for Standard and Professional editions).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83a24d82-c890-4d54-9bba-50ac5c213ddd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Additional custom types of transformations can be defined by plugins.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737014 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02421: (2026x) Avoiding Conflicting Legend items

- page_id: `272737014`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737014/2026x+Avoiding+Conflicting+Legend+items
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:14.158+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Legends > (2026x) Creating Legends > (2026x) Specifying Legend Items
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you use multiple Legends in one diagram, you should specify a different set of adornment properties for Legend Items of each Legend. This way, Legend Items of different Legends do not conflict when applied to the same element. For example, try using **Fill Color** in one Legend and **Icon** in another. If you want to use the **Icon** property in multiple Legends, then specify different **Icon Position on Shape**/**Path** for Legend Items of each Legend. In addition, you can define Legend Item adornment by specifying the **Pen Color**, **Line Width,** and **Line Style** properties describing the appearance of a shape border or path.

#### TIP: Legend Item priority

Legend Item priority

If you can not avoid conflicting Legend Items in a diagram or table, specify the **Priority** property for each Legend Item. It defines which Legend Item should be applied in case several Legend Items conflict.

##### Example

Let's say you want to use two Legends in a Requirements Diagram: the *Status* Legend indicating the status of each Requirement and the *Risk* Legend showing the risk level of Requirements. It is likely that Legend Items from both Legends will be applied to the same Requirement. For example, a single Requirement can have both status and risk values specified. Let's analyze this specific use case by creating the Legends mentioned above and defining their Legend Item adornment properties so that Legend Items would not conflict when applied to the same Requirement:

1. Open the desired Requirements Diagram.
2. In the diagram, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Legends' space=''] intended to show the status of Requirements and name the Legend Status .
3. Create and define the Legend Items for the Status Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''] indicating four possible Requirement status values and name them Closed , Approved , Disapproved, and In Progress .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired Fill Color .
  4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying Legend Items' space='']Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the Status property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.
4. In the same diagram, create a Legend to indicate the risk level of Requirements and name the Legend Risk .
5. Create and define the Legend Items for the Risk Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. Create three Legend Items indicating three possible Requirement risk levels and name them High , Medium , and Low .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired **Icon** and **Icon Position on Shape**. Specifying IconWhen specifying the **Icon** property:Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select an image from the Image Library of your modeling tool.Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select a custom image from the local file system.Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to specify the URL of an image.
  4. Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the **Risk** property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.

If you followed the above steps, you have a Requirements Diagram with the *Status* and *Risk* Legends applied to it. The Legend Items of both Legends do not conflict when applied to the same shape, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The figure displays a Requirement shape with two Legend Items applied to it. 
The Legend Items do not conflict due to different adornment properties used to define them.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This way, Legend Items of different Legends do not conflict when applied to the same element. For example, try using <strong>Fill Color</strong> in one Legend and <strong>Icon</strong> in another. If you want to use the <strong>Icon</strong> property in multiple Legends, then specify different <strong>Icon Position on Shape</strong>/<strong>Path</strong> for Legend Items of each Legend. In addition, you can define Legend Item adornment by specifying the <strong>Pen Color</strong>, <strong>Line Width,</strong> and <strong>Line Style</strong> properties describing the appearance of a shape border or path.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ade7b350-1fd3-44cc-9bdc-bf32eb39765d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Legend Item priority</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you can not avoid conflicting Legend Items in a diagram or table, specify the <strong>Priority</strong> property for each Legend Item. property:</p><ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image_library.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Avoiding Conflicting Legend items" by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the <strong>Risk</strong> property value the Legend Item represents.</p></li><li>Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.</li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you followed the above steps, you have a Requirements Diagram with the <em>Status</em> and <em>Risk</em> Legends applied to it. The Legend Items of both Legends do not conflict when applied to the same shape, as shown below.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_adornment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Avoiding Conflicting Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">The figure displays a Requirement shape with two Legend Items applied to it.</span><br style="text-align: center;" /><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">The Legend Items do not conflict due to different adornment properties used to define them.</span></h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737943 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02422: (2026x) Background Task Manager

- page_id: `272737943`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737943/2026x+Background+Task+Manager
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:25.219+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Improving performance
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1427371678

1427371681

1427371679

Low-priority or routine tasks are moved to separate threads and are performed as background tasks. These tasks do not stop a modeling tool from running, and you can work with your model as usual. Background tasks are displayed in the Background Task Manager at the bottom right corner of the modeling tool window. For example, when a search process is running, the progress bar is displayed in the Background Task Manager, shown in the following figure.

If a background task runs for too long, you can cancel it as described below.

To end an ongoing process

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='end_process.png'] in the Background Task Manager status bar to cancel the selected process. If you cancel the last of several tasks (or only one existing process), the Background Task Manager is turned off.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Background Task Manager showing the progress of a searching task.

1427371677

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Improving performance' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Memory monitor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Memory allocation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Active validation period' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0489cd22-1695-4672-842f-610250289d3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427371678</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3676a67-3017-4c37-b3e2-c8095fc9764f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427371681</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d9754e7-9411-4e55-bb68-866572376638"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427371679</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Low-priority or routine tasks are moved to separate threads and are performed as background tasks. These tasks do not stop a modeling tool from running, and you can work with your model as usual. Background tasks are displayed in the Background Task Manager at the bottom right corner of the modeling tool window. For example, when a search process is running, the progress bar is displayed in the Background Task Manager, shown in the following figure.</p><p>If a background task runs for too long, you can cancel it as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To end an ongoing process</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="end_process.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Background Task Manager status bar to cancel the selected process. If you cancel the last of several tasks (or only one existing process), the Background Task Manager is turned off.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="progress_bar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Background Task Manager" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Background Task Manager showing the progress of a searching task.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6114c90-5668-43eb-a1ec-3efcad6b5ae9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427371677</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Improving performance" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Memory monitor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Memory allocation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Active validation period" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 02423: (2026x) Backward traceability – specification

- page_id: `272735441`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735441/2026x+Backward+traceability+%E2%80%93+specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:23.754+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Traceability > (2026x) Predefined traceability rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| Property Name | Description | Applied For: | Reference Through: | Value elements type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Manifested in Artifacts | The property shows which components are manifested in an artifact. | Artifact | Relationships: Manifestation | Component | Example |
| Specifying Class | The property shows the classes representing the component specification from the Design model. | Component | Relationships: Abstraction | Class | Example |
| Specifying Component | The property shows the components that are realized by classifiers through component realization. | Classifier | Relationships: Component Realization, Realization | Component | Example |
| Specifying Use Case | The property shows the use cases (from the Requirements model) representing the class specification. | Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Specifying Use Case | The property shows the use cases that specify the given use case in the higher level of abstraction. For example, the Business Use Case specifies the Requirements Use Case. | Use Case | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Realized Interface | The property shows the interfaces specifying the contract, in which the related classifier conforms to. | Classifier | Relationships: Interface Realization | Interface | Example |
| Specifying Element, All Specifying ElementsAvailable in Enterprise and Architect editions only. | The Specifying Element property gathers specifying elements from the upper abstraction level. The All Specifying Elements property transitively gathers specifying elements from all upper abstraction levels. | Element | Relationships: Abstraction, Component Realization, Interface Realization. | Element | Example |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739285 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02424: (2026x) Basic concepts

- page_id: `272739285`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739285/2026x+Basic+concepts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:12.177+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Data-related modeling > (2026x) Entity-relationship (ER) modeling and diagrams
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An entity is a thing capable of an existence. An entity usually refers to some aspect of the real world, which can be distinguished from other aspects of the real world (a person, place, customer transaction, order, etc.).

An entity is represented by a box shape on the diagram. An entity has two compartments where properties (columns) of the entity can be specified. The upper compartment holds the primary key properties of the entity; the lower compartment holds other properties.

A relationship between entities describes how entities are associated. Relationships are represented by lines, connecting entities. Relationship end adornments indicate multiplicities of these ends. Multiplicity is the number of entity instances that can be associated with a number of another entity instances. Relationship multiplicity is represented by three symbols, the so-called “crow's foot notation” or “Information Engineering notation”.

The following table shows symbols of the relationship multiplicity.

| Name | Value | Notation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Zero | Zero |  |
| Vertical | One |  |
| Crow's foot | Many |  |

Multiplicity lower bounds and upper bounds are paired into one adornment, as shown in the following table. Note that any lower bound, which is more that 0 is treated as 1 (this also includes lower bounds greater than 1, such as 2.). Also, any upper bound which is greater than 1 is treated as Many (this also includes upper bounds less than unlimited, such as 7.).

| Min | Max | Read as | Figure |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | 1 | One (optional) |  |
| 1 | 1 | One (mandatory) |  |
| 0 | Many | Many (optional) |  |
| 1 | Many | Many (mandatory) |  |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Basic ER diagram example.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- Some authors use Entity Type term to signify the description of a set of entities and Entity Instance term to signify concrete exemplar from that set. Entity term used in this manual corresponds to Entity Type .
- Data modeling world frequently uses term Cardinality to denote the allowable numbers of entity instances, which can be associated. But with the rise of the UML, the more correct term Multiplicity was introduced and term Cardinality is only used to denote concrete numbers of entity instances, which are associated. Hence in the example Person [0..1]------
- -[0..*] Book, the ranges [0..1] and [0..*] are called multiplicities. And if we have person “John Doe” associated with books “Moby Dick” and “Origin of Species”, we have a cardinality of 2 for loaned books role (and 1 on an opposite end - current reader role). Note that cardinality is always concrete number while multiplicity denotes range of possible cardinalities.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

An entity has two compartments where properties (columns) of the entity can be specified. The upper compartment holds the primary key properties of the entity; the lower compartment holds other properties.</p><p>A relationship between entities describes how entities are associated. Relationships are represented by lines, connecting entities. Relationship end adornments indicate multiplicities of these ends. Multiplicity is the number of entity instances that can be associated with a number of another entity instances. Relationship multiplicity is represented by three symbols, the so-called “crow's foot notation” or “Information Engineering notation”.</p><p>The following table shows symbols of the relationship multiplicity.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 69.9945%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 42.9451%;" /><col style="width: 24.9216%;" /><col style="width: 32.1333%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: left;">Name</th><th style="text-align: left;">Value</th><th style="text-align: left;">Notation</th></tr><tr><td>Zero</td><td>Zero</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Zero Notation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic concepts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>Vertical</td><td>One</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Vertical Notation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic concepts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Crow's foot</p></td><td>Many</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Crow's Foot Notation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic concepts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p>Multiplicity lower bounds and upper bounds are paired into one adornment, as shown in the following table. Note that any lower bound, which is more that 0 is treated as 1 (this also includes lower bounds greater than 1, such as 2.). ac:macro-id="b767ba70-1442-4a55-8ae0-36e002775508"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Some authors use <strong>Entity Type </strong>term to signify the description of a set of entities and <strong>Entity </strong><strong>Instance </strong>term to signify concrete exemplar from that set. <strong>Entity </strong>term used in this manual corresponds to And if we have person “John Doe” associated with books “Moby Dick” and “Origin of Species”, we have a cardinality of 2 for loaned books role (and 1 on an opposite end - current reader role). Note that cardinality is always concrete number while multiplicity denotes range of possible cardinalities.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729548 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02427: (2026x) Basic tasks in tables

- page_id: `272729548`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729548/2026x+Basic+tasks+in+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:35:21.047+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Important

218215203

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Important

218215208

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Important

218215207

Tables are diagrams which allow you to manage any amount of model data in a spreadsheet-like form. It is much easier to analyze a group of data in one place, especially when you can create new elements, add existing elements, edit their properties directly from the table, and paste data from the clipboard. This allows you to sort and filter data, show, hide, reorder columns, and clear or edit individual cells. Exporting tables into more commonly used file formats, such as *.html*, *.csv*, or *.xlsx*, makes it simple to share them with stakeholders.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

Each table row represents an element. Each column represents element properties. The column header and row number header are highlighted in grey when selecting single or multiple cells.

The following procedures provide the basic tasks to get you started working with all modeling tool tables:

218215198

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Legends' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Editing property values' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Exporting tables into more commonly used file formats, such as <em>.html</em>, <em>.csv</em>, or <em>.xlsx</em>, makes it simple to share them with stakeholders.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b042c13b-e2a7-4c98-ac3a-2112c07745ec"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Each table row represents an element. Each column represents element properties. The column header and row number header are highlighted in grey when selecting single or multiple cells.<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> The following procedures provide the basic tasks to get you started working with all modeling tool tables:</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c028ddb8-cd2a-486c-bc76-8b88a7ef53ee" /><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="069bf8cb-0f0f-47d2-9d45-c8cae942f62b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">218215198</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Legends" /></ac:link></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Editing property values" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731963 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02428: (2026x) Behavior to structure synchronization

- page_id: `272731963`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731963/2026x+Behavior+to+structure+synchronization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:56.699+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1084440424

1084440437

1084440427

The behavior to structure synchronization checks the consistency of the Internal Block diagram (IBD) with Activities, automatically detects inconsistencies and enables users to handle each inconsistency individually by providing solvers. As a result, the coherence of IBDs with Activities is ensured at any stage of the model development process.

The behavior to structure synchronization validates the Part Properties displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] to check for compatible

- Proxy Ports
- Flow Properties
- Interface Blocks
- Connectors
- Item Flows

To verify whether or not the structural elements (e.g., a Proxy Port and an Interface Block) and behavioral elements (e.g., a flow) are compatible, validation rules are used. If any inconsistencies are found, they are highlighted in yellow in the Internal Block Diagram and displayed in the**Validation Results** panel. Then, they can be individually handled using solvers.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing behavior to structure.

##### Checking behavior to structure synchronization

You need to set the **Allocation Mode**(**Options**>**Project**>**SysML**>**Allocation Mode**)project optionto **Usage** before you can synchronize behavior to structure.

To check the structure model consistency with the behavior model

1. In the toolbar of the Internal Block Diagram, click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools.
2. In the open drop-down menu, select Behavior to Structure Synchronization. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The behavior to structure synchronization check is initiated.

484219123

**Continue reading**

</span><strong>SysML</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>Allocation Mode</strong><span>) </span>project option<span> </span>to <strong>Usage</strong> before you can synchronize behavior to structure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To check the structure model consistency with the behavior model</p><hr /><ol><li>In the toolbar of the Internal Block Diagram, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Tools.</li><li>In the open drop-down menu, select <strong>Behavior to Structure Synchronization.</strong><br /><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_to_structure_sync_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></li></ol><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a4344bbf-9a03-49f3-8337-2857606c756f">The behavior to structure synchronization check is initiated.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b67cc1f-75c3-42fa-b535-cd642921b424"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">484219123</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Continue reading</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3cb859a5-2ed8-42c2-b553-a77c7e0bf462" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732369 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02429: (2026x) Blackbox ICD Table

- page_id: `272732369`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732369/2026x+Blackbox+ICD+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:31.442+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) SysML diagram descriptions
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Blackbox ICD Table represents all external Ports/interfaces of the selected Block. The table is useful when trying to identify what kind of inputs and outputs are available in the selected system.

In the following example, the distiller system has two inputs (*dirty water*and *q in*) and three outputs (*bypass*, *purified* and *sludge*). In this case, the Port *dirty water*, Port *purified*, Port *bypass* support such value properties as: *water temp*, *water press*, etc. Pay attention, that the input to distiller is a *dirty water* (row 1) and the output from distiller is *purified*water (row 4). Both of them contains the same properties but it may have different values defined (they are not defined in the figure). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Interface Control Document tables' space='']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Blackbox ICD Table of the Distiller Block.

A Blackbox ICD Table consists of the following columns.

| Column name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| # | A row number. |
| ~ ^ | Displays if Port is conjugated (~) or inherited (^). |
| Port Name | A Port name. |
| Port Type | A Port type. |
| Type Features | Displays all the features (e.g., Flow property, Part property) of the Port type. |
| Direction | Displays a combined direction prefix of the Port. |
| Documentation | Displays a documentation text. If it is not described, click to edit. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736166 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02430: (2026x) Block structure

- page_id: `272736166`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736166/2026x+Block+structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:49.274+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Sample models > (2026x) Creating a simple model > (2026x) Blocks and Parts
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The three components are connected through ports that correspond electrical pins. The source and the resistor each have both a positive and a negative pin, whereas the ground has only one positive pin. Electrical energy (carried by electric charge) is transmitted between components through the pins. The image below contains the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] that illustrates this.

A new abstract block *TwoPinComponent* is created to illustrate abstract components that have two ports (pins). These two ports are named *p*******and*n*, and they are both of the *ChargeFlowElement*type. ChargeFlowElement belongs to the simulation library (*SysPhSLibrary.mdzip*), and it can be selected by unchecking **Apply Filter**in the type list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *p* and*n* ports typed by *ChargeFlow Element*Interface Block.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The three components are connected through ports that correspond electrical pins. The source and the resistor each have both a positive and a negative pin, whereas the ground has only one positive pin. Electrical energy (carried by electric charge) is transmitted between components through the pins. The image below contains the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Block Definition Diagram (BDD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that illustrates this. </p><p>A new abstract block <em>TwoPinComponent</em> is created to illustrate abstract components that have two ports (pins). These two ports are named <em>p</em><strong><em> </em> </strong>and<em> n</em>, and they are both of the <em>ChargeFlowElement </em>type. ChargeFlowElement belongs to the simulation library (<em>SysPhSLibrary.mdzip</em>), and it can be selected by unchecking <strong>Apply Filter  </strong>in the type list. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="470"><ri:attachment ri:filename="electrical_components.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Block structure" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">The <em>p</em> and<em> n</em> ports typed by <em>ChargeFlow Element </em>Interface Block.</span></h6><p><br /></p><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732339 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02431: (2026x) Blocks and Parts

- page_id: `272732339`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732339/2026x+Blocks+and+Parts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:27.828+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Sample models > (2026x) Creating a simple model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1553744160

1553744160

1553744160

The electrical circuit model has the main *block* and the*circuit*. To model the structure of the circuit, create a Circuit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] and an associated [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']. The circuit has three parts: *a source*, *a ground*, and *a resistor*. These parts are of a different type since they all have a different behavior. It is necessary to create a Block for each of these parts. The picture below shows how the Internal Block Diagram look like.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The *Circuit* IBD consists of three parts: *source*, *ground* and *resistor*.

1553744159

**Related pages:**

****

To model the structure of the circuit, create a Circuit <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> and an associated <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Internal Block Diagram (IBD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The circuit has three parts: <em>a source</em>, <em>a ground</em>, and <em>a resistor</em>. These parts are of a different type since they all have a different behavior. It is necessary to create a Block for each of these parts. The picture below shows how the Internal Block Diagram look like. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ibd_sample_model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Blocks and Parts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The <em>Circuit</em> IBD consists of three parts: <em>source</em>, <em>ground</em> and <em>resistor</em>.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a28dfc3c-f9c4-4c32-b5d8-8f5dd784e13d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1553744159</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages:</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b30fcee2-0b0c-40d0-92aa-37f61062237b" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 02432: (2026x) Bookmark tool

- page_id: `272737609`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737609/2026x+Bookmark+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:49.267+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2050367121

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2050367135

2050367115

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050367125

$bookmark contains utility functions for bookmarking. The functions of this module are accessible from templates through **$bookmark**.

#### INFO: Information

Information

The default style of bookmarks depends on the editor used. For example, Microsoft Word 2003 displays hyperlinks in blue.

****

###### **$bookmark.openURL(url, content)**

Create a hyperlink to open a URL.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | url | java.lang.String | A URL text. |
| content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

****

###### **$bookmark.openURL(url, content)**

Create a hyperlink to open a URL.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | url | java.net.URL | A URL instance. |
| content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

****

###### **$bookmark.openURL(uri, content)**

Create a hyperlink to open a URI.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | uri | java.net.URI | A URI instance. |
| content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

****

###### **$bookmark.open(content)**

Create a hyperlink for a bookmark. The bookmark ID will be automatically generated from the content.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

****

###### **$bookmark.open(bookmarkId, content)**

Create a hyperlink for a bookmark. The bookmark ID value must match the parameter bookmark ID that passes onto the link.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkId | java.lang.String | The bookmark ID. |
|  | content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

###### **$bookmark.create(bookmarkObject)**

Create a bookmark. The bookmark ID will be automatically generated from the bookmark object.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkObject | java.lang.Object | A bookmark object or content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The content with a bookmark. |

****

###### **$bookmark.create(bookmarkId, bookmarkObject)**

Create a bookmark. The default element type for this function is "label".

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkId | java.lang.String | The bookmark ID. |
|  | bookmarkObject | java.lang.Object | A bookmark object or content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The content with a bookmark. |

For example:

```text

```

###### **$bookmark.create(bookmarkId, bookmarkObject, elementType)**

Create a bookmark by specifying an element type.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkId | java.lang.String | The bookmark ID. |
|  | bookmarkObject | java.lang.Object | A bookmark object or content. |
|  | elementType | java.lang.String | An element type name, e.g., html tag. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The content with a bookmark. |

****

###### **$bookmark.getBookmarkId(id)**

Return a bookmark ID from a given string.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | id | java.lang.String | An original string value. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The bookmark ID. |

****

###### **$bookmark.createRef(referenceID)**

Create a reference bookmark of any Microsoft Word reference numbered items.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | referenceID | java.lang.String | A reference ID. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.TextReference | The content with a bookmark in the Microsoft Word format. |

****

###### **$bookmark.openRef(referenceID)**

Create a hyperlink for a reference bookmark of any Microsoft Word reference numbered items.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | referenceID | java.lang.String | A reference ID. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.TextReference | The content with a bookmark in the Microsoft Word format. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can press F9 to replace the text content shown as **Refer** with the updated numbered item that it refers to.

****

###### **$bookmark.openRef(referenceID, referenceText)**

Create a hyperlink for a reference bookmark of any Microsoft Word reference numbered items.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | referenceID | java.lang.String | A reference ID. |
|  | referenceText | java.lang.String | The text content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.TextReference | The content with a bookmark in the Microsoft Word format. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can press F9 to replace the text content shown as **Refer referenceText** with the updated numbered item that it refers to.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738361 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02433: (2026x) Boundary Events

- page_id: `272738361`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738361/2026x+Boundary+Events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:01.480+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Boundary Event is an Intermediate event which can be placed on the boundary of any of the following activities:

- SubProcess, Task, or Call Activity
- SubChoreography, Choreography Task, or Call Choreography

Boundary Event indicates that while attached-to Activity is running, event is listening for the trigger signal.

Boundary Event types:

- Interrupting Boundary Event aborts Activity and Process is continued of exceptional flow. This event is drawn with a solid border.
- Non-Interrupting Boundary Event splits process to parallel flows. This event is drawn with a dashed border.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Error Boundary Event attached to SubProcess

Types of Boundary Events are as follows:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Boundary Event is an Intermediate event which can be placed on the boundary of any of the following activities:</p><ul><li>SubProcess, Task, or Call Activity</li><li>SubChoreography, Choreography Task, or Call Choreography</li></ul><p>Boundary Event indicates that while attached-to Activity is running, event is listening for the trigger signal.</p><p>Boundary Event types:</p><ul><li>Interrupting Boundary Event aborts Activity and Process is continued of exceptional flow. This event is drawn with a solid border.</li><li>Non-Interrupting Boundary Event splits process to parallel flows. This event is drawn with a dashed border.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="boundary_error.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Boundary Events" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Error Boundary Event attached to SubProcess</h6><p>Types of Boundary Events are as follows:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="dd5528d2-89f7-4470-8751-3f740afa86c7" /></p>
````

## PAGE 02434: (2026x) BPMN Activities Description Table

- page_id: `272729199`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729199/2026x+BPMN+Activities+Description+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:46:53.110+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Tables
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A BPMN Activities Description table is designed to display or edit selected process activities descriptions in a table form. A BPMN Activities Description table is created for a BPMN Process.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Activities Description table

**Related elements**

- Activities
- BPMN Tables

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 02435: (2026x) BPMN Association

- page_id: `272739040`
- space_key: `MT`

- version_number: 2

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x) Business Data Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487823057

1487823057

1487823057

**Description**

A BPMN Association link shows that particular classes are related to each other. BPMN Association links may have names defined – names help to read diagrams. You can also specify multiplicity at the end of a BPMN Association.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Association between two Classes with Multiplicity at the End

1487823056

**Related element**

- Class

**Related diagram**

- Business Data Diagram

## PAGE 02436: (2026x) BPMN Business Data Description Table

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A BPMN Business Data Description table is designed to display or edit class descriptions in a table form. You can display existing classes or create new classes in this table.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Business Data Description table

**Related elements**

- Class
- BPMN Tables
