## PAGE 02437: (2026x) BPMN Choreography Diagram

- page_id: `272738114`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738114/2026x+BPMN+Choreography+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:45:44.697+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486494018

1486494019

1486494019

**Description**

A Choreography formalizes the way business participants coordinate their interactions. A Choreography is a type of process, but its purpose and behavior are different from a standard BPMN process.

A standard process defines the flow of activities of a specific partner entity or organization. In contrast, a Choreography formalizes the way business participants coordinate their interactions. The focus is not on the work performed within these participants, but rather on the information (messages) exchanged between them.

A Choreography is a definition of expected behavior, basically a procedural business contract between interacting participants. It shows the messages exchanged and their logical relations. This allows business partners to plan their business processes for inter-operation without introducing conflicts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Choreography diagram

A BPMN Choreography diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram and includes restrictions and extensions as defined by BPMN.

Types of Choreography elements are as follows:

1486494017

**Related elements**

- Choreography
- Choreography Activities
- Error
- Intermediate Catch Event
- Intermediate Throwing Event
- Boundary Events
- End Events
- Gateways

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Process Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Using BPMN Choreography Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738983 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02438: (2026x) BPMN Collaboration Diagram

- page_id: `272738983`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738983/2026x+BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:45:34.376+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486122303

1486122304

1486122304

**Description**

A Collaboration represents the interactions between two or more business entities. A Collaboration diagram depicts a global point of view. It shows the interactions between participants in general.

A Collaboration contains two or more pools, representing the participants in the collaboration. Messages exchanged between the participants are shown by message flows that connect two pools together (or objects within the pools).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaboration diagram with two Black Box Pools

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaboration diagram with two Pools showing process

A Collaboration diagram can also show distinct conversations between collaborating participants in a domain. Communications are defined by the conversations, participants, and conversation links between them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Collaboration diagram with Conversations

The elements of a BPMN Process diagram can be displayed on the BPMN Collaboration diagram.

1486122303

**Related element**

- BPMN Process

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Process Diagram

## PAGE 02439: (2026x) BPMN Data Usage Matrices

- page_id: `272729212`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729212/2026x+BPMN+Data+Usage+Matrices
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:46:57.660+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Matrices
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A BPMN Data Usage Matrix shows what data classes are used in BPMN Processes and SubProcess. Data Objects represents classes in a BPMN diagram.

A BPMN Data Usage Matrix presents information about all BPMN Processes and SubProcesses in rows and Classes in columns. Each row in the matrix shows if a class is used in a BPMN Process or SubProcess diagram as a Data Object.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Data Usage Matrix

**Related elements**

- BPMN Process
- SubProcesses
- Class
- BPMN Matrices

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Process Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']
- Dependency Matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Data Objects represents classes in a BPMN diagram.</p><p>A BPMN Data Usage Matrix presents information about all BPMN Processes and SubProcesses in rows and Classes in columns. Each row in the matrix shows if a class is used in a BPMN Process or SubProcess diagram as a Data Object.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_usage_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) BPMN Data Usage Matrices" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>BPMN Data Usage Matrix</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="8a678172-4e84-4051-93e8-3ad7905acea2"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Process">BPMN Process</a></li><li data-uuid="3b1e47f1-ae45-4def-8135-3ec2cc6c6c12"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) SubProcesses">SubProcesses</a></li><li data-uuid="adb9546d-6105-42b1-9925-dcc64e6b07fe"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Class">Class</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="64d5c999-ac19-4c28-9ccc-9b493db1eca3"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Matrices">BPMN Matrices</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="1c264b88-0dcb-4684-b635-9527890d142d"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="076434e6-dc42-4c53-9a5c-4af2e2c40594"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating a Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="79c6266a-9c9c-44a4-9d26-878d3510dc3a"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Dependency+Matrix">Dependency Matrix</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729146 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02441: (2026x) BPMN modeling

- page_id: `272729146`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729146/2026x+BPMN+modeling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:53.987+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Cameo Business Modeler Plugin provides implementation of the Business Process Modeling and Notation (BPMN) standard and the best capability for understanding internal business procedures. The notation includes performance, collaboration, and business transactions in your business between organizations. For service providers and consultants, Cameo Business Modeler Plugin is also the ideal communication tool. Not only do you get the benefits of a standard business notation, but also many others that you and your customer/stakeholder can trust and understand. With collaboration and reporting tools, you can integrate your customer right into your process.

To learn more, see the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">The Cameo Business Modeler Plugin provides implementation of the Business Process Modeling and Notation (BPMN) standard and the best capability for understanding internal business procedures. The notation includes performance, collaboration, and business transactions in your business between organizations. For service providers and consultants, Cameo Business Modeler Plugin is also the ideal communication tool. Not only do you get the benefits of a standard business notation, but also many others that you and your customer/stakeholder can trust and understand. With collaboration and reporting tools, you can integrate your customer right into your process.</p><p style="text-align: left;">To learn more, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d054600b-d5b5-4386-b9be-1f7c6f0d6366" /></p>
````

**Description**

A BPMN Process element defines a process performed in an organization. This element is a container for the BPMN Process diagram and its elements. Process diagram describes how a process is performed.

1485168491

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729150 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02443: (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram

- page_id: `272729150`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729150/2026x+BPMN+Process+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:44:17.345+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A BPMN Process Diagram describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business works. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities. A BPMN Process Diagram is similar to the UML Activity diagram with a much richer set of default message types and business process styles of notations.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Process diagram

**Related elements**

- BPMN Process
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A BPMN Process Diagram describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business works. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities. A BPMN Process Diagram is similar to the UML Activity diagram with a much richer set of default message types and business process styles of notations.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="process_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) BPMN Process Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">BPMN Process diagram</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="cc08b33f-4bd0-477c-8df8-9cd086810848"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Process">BPMN Process</a></li><li data-uuid="821cbae5-3519-47f0-bbb3-7d94a4a2051c"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating a Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735785 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02445: (2026x) BPMN Processes Structure Map

- page_id: `272735785`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735785/2026x+BPMN+Processes+Structure+Map
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:46:58.850+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Supportive Diagrams Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

148567171

148567172

148567172

**Description**

A BPMN Processes Structure Map diagram allows you to represent the structure of processes in a project. The 
processes are grouped into packages on the diagram. And each process may itself contains other processes, 
which in turn may contain other processes. The diagram makes the processes easier to understand and helps you 
analyze how they are organized.

A BPMN Processes Structure Map diagram shows packages, processes, subprocesses, and tasks from a 
selected scope.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Processes Structure Map

148567170

**Related elements**

- Package
- BPMN Process
- Activities

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Process Diagram

**Related external resource**

- Relation Map

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729191 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02447: (2026x) BPMN Resources Description Table

- page_id: `272729191`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729191/2026x+BPMN+Resources+Description+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:46:50.034+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Tables
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A BPMN Resources Description table is designed to display or edit resources description in a table form. You can display existing Resources or create new ones in this table.

BPMN defines four types of Resources as follows:

- Resource
- Organization Unit
- Role
- Person
- Information System

You can display all of them in a BPMN Resources Description table.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Resource Roles Description table

**Related elements**

- Resource
- BPMN Tables

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A BPMN Resources Description table is designed to display or edit resources description in a table form. You can display existing Resources or create new ones in this table.</p><p>BPMN defines four types of Resources as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="11ed7d5d-dde7-4511-b77e-4a37222ffdad">Resource</li><li data-uuid="cbadfedd-55c5-4b68-8784-8e6e809782fd">Organization Unit</li><li data-uuid="85db651e-8974-46d5-8528-ad27739b323b">Role</li><li data-uuid="e2aff2e1-d9f6-4e6d-90e6-8596a965c5a3">Person</li><li data-uuid="d1465541-b301-4bd1-8f50-c8ec47954ddc">Information System</li></ul><p>You can display all of them in a BPMN Resources Description table.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_description_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) BPMN Resources Description Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>BPMN Resource Roles Description table</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="97cfb87c-bccb-4781-939e-057e40d7afcd"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Resource">Resource</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="cbe23566-5b8d-45a7-a19c-aeff59a1f6b6"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Tables">BPMN Tables</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="db17430e-d860-40c0-9238-14a9e6030ea7"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating a Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729196 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02448: (2026x) BPMN Resources Usage Matrices

- page_id: `272729196`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729196/2026x+BPMN+Resources+Usage+Matrices
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:46:56.317+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Matrices
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A BPMN Resources Usage Matrix is designed to review and edit an allocation of Resource Roles for BPMN Activities. The Resource Roles used in an Activity show a Resources property in a BPMN Activity specification dialog.

A BPMN Resources Usage Matrix presents information about BPMN Activities in rows and Resource Roles in columns. Each arrow in the matrix shows if a Resource Role is used by an Activity. 
You can define Resource Roles usage for all BPMN Activities by assigning or removing the Resource Roles to or from the Activities.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Resource Usage Matrix

**Related elements**

- BPMN Process
- Activities
- Resource
- Organization Unit
- Role
- Person
- Information system
- BPMN Matrices

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']
- Dependency Matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Types of BPMN tables in Cameo Business Modeler are as follows:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739307 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02450: (2026x) BPMN2 XML support

- page_id: `272739307`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739307/2026x+BPMN2+XML+support
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:45:56.521+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486966503

1486966503

1486966503

Cameo Business Modeler supports exporting BPMN2 models as BPMN2 Diagram Interchange files. The BPMN2 Diagram Interchange is a format for interchanging BPMN2 diagrams between tools. Its reduced ambiguity allows the users to exchange BPMN models between tools more conveniently. You can export a BPMN2 diagram as a BPMN2 Diagram Interchange file (BPMN2 XMI).

1486966502

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Exporting Models to BPMN2

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The BPMN2 Diagram Interchange is a format for interchanging BPMN2 diagrams between tools. Its reduced ambiguity allows the users to exchange BPMN models between tools more conveniently. You can export a BPMN2 diagram as a BPMN2 Diagram Interchange file (BPMN2 XMI).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfc7d778-00b2-4416-93a0-b9afd287a5bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486966502</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Exporting+Models+to+BPMN2">Exporting Models to BPMN2</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486303594

1486303595

1486303595

**Description**

A BPMNMessage represents the content of communications between two participants. It is passed by a message flow and is sent or received by a message event.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1486303593

**Related elements**

- Message Flow
- Choreography Task

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9efdbe25-80c2-42df-9e43-1ba6f6cd8c54"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486303594</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5bb96a63-33d4-44dd-8cf8-0302f7718ca0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486303595</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2fe4e6a-016b-497f-bba9-df8a92bc8788"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486303595</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A BPMNMessage represents the content of communications between two participants. It is passed by a message flow and is sent or received by a message event.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="message.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) BPMNMessage" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0244fb7-b779-45b3-aad6-6b442b33b8df"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486303593</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Message+Flow">Message Flow</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Choreography+Task">Choreography Task</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Creating+and+Using+Message+Flow">Creating and Using Message Flow</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Creating+and+Using+Choreography+Task">Creating and Using Choreography Task</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731925 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02452: (2026x) Building AcV-1 matrix

- page_id: `272731925`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731925/2026x+Building+AcV-1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:50.722+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an OV-4 before creating an AcV-1.

The AcV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) for Actual Projects.

The rows in the AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) and the columns represent Actual Projects.

To build an AcV-1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an OV-4 before creating an AcV-1.</p><p>The AcV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) for Actual Projects.</p><p>The rows in the AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) and the columns represent Actual Projects.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build an AcV-1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736771 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02453: (2026x) Building metric definitions

- page_id: `272736771`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736771/2026x+Building+metric+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:40.707+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools > (2026x) Metric Suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1891786832

1891786834

1891786833

Metric definition is the attribute of a Metric Suite with the «MetricDefinition» stereotype applied.

To build a metric definition

1. Select the shape of a Metric Suite.
2. Click the Create Element button on the shape, and then select Metric Definition . An attribute with the «MetricDefinition» stereotype is created. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_metric_definition.png']
3. Type its name directly on the shape and then click a free space on the diagram.
4. Select the attribute on the shape and click [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_type_button.png'] .
5. Select the type of the result values that should be calculated according to the formula specified in a metric definition. This step is mandatory. A metric definition must have a result type, e.g., Real or Integer.
6. Press **Enter** to open the Specification window of the metric definition.
7. Click the cell of the Default Value property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] and [ATTACHMENT filename='show_shortcut_menu.png'] appear on the cell.
8. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show_shortcut_menu.png'] and select Value Specification > Opaque Expression .
9. Click the cell of the **Default Value** property value again, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Default Value** dialog opens.
10. From the **Language** drop-down list, select a language. To build a structured expression, select **StructuredExpression**.
11. In the **Body** box, specify the formula for the metric definition. For more information about building structured expressions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''].
12. Click OK and then click Close in the Specification window.

The metric definition is specified.

If your Metric Suite is created using the *BaseMetricSuite*, it already has the metric definition for date tracking.

One metric definition can refer to another metric definition of either the same Metric Suite or a more general one (in case there is the Generalization relationship between these Metric Suites).

1891786831

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Metric Suites' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding a new Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specify the target for the Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying parameter definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specific use cases' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Building validation-based metric definitions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731587 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02454: (2026x) Building Modelica libraries in SysML v1

- page_id: `272731587`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731587/2026x+Building+Modelica+libraries+in+SysML+v1
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:55.172+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Modelica export
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Introduction

The Modelica library allows quickly to build SysML v1 models for simulation by reusing library elements. It provides model components in many domains that are based on standardized interface definitions. The library components are ready to use and shareable between applications. [Find all modelica libraries >>](https://www.modelica.org/libraries)

##### Importing the Modelica Standard Library

The Modelica Standard Library brings the maincomponentsand standard component interfaces from many engineering domains. It provides theblack-boxes of Modelica blocks that contains only ports and parameters.The Modelica Standard Library components are imported to SysML v1 project as follow:

- All Modelica types have «ModelicaBlock» stereotype applied with a full Modelica type name specified in the Name property.
- Modelica blocks are created as SysML Blocks.
- Modelica parameters are created as SysML Value Properties.
- Modelica connectors are created as SysML Interface Blocks.
- Primitive types are created as Value Types.
- If Modelica connector has property with flow keyword defined, it is created as SysML Flow Property for an Interface Block.
- For Block (defined as Model in Modelica) import and create only ports (no keyword, type is Modelica Connector/SysML InterfaceBlock) and parameters as ValueProperties.
- Units and quantities are mapped to ISO80000 units and quantities.

All modelica libraries depend from the Modelica Standard Library because of references between components.That's why the first thing you nee to do before using Modelica libraries in SysML v1 projects, is to import the Modelica Standard Library.

impTo import Modelica Standard Library

1. [Download](https://github.com/modelica/ModelicaStandardLibrary/releases) the Modelica Standard Library and extract it.
2. Open a project in which you want to use the Modelica library components.
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**> **Modelica Library**.
4. Select the *ModelicaStandardLibrary > Modelica* folder from your file system.
5. Click **Import**.The Modelica Standard Library is imported. See the following figure.All packages with their structure is createdin the Containment treeand types are placed in corresponding packages. Note, that file names may do not match with the element names and the file structure may do not correspond to the location in the directory. The package structure is the same as Modelica package structure. In order to to avoid the import of Modelica Standard Library in each project, you can save it as a project and reuse in other projects. For this, do the following:[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Sharing project data' space='']>]]>[https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Sharing+project+data](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Sharing+project+data)[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using other projects in a project' space='']>]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Using Modelica libraries

Let's say we need to use *OpenHydraulics* modelica library components in a project. Both *Modelica Standard Library* and *OpenHydraulics* library**must be imported since some components from OpenHydraulics library have references to standard library.

To use the Modelica libraries in SysML v1 project

1. Create new or open existing SysML v1 project.
2. Import the Modelica Standard Library . >]]>
3. [Download](https://github.com/modelica-3rdparty/OpenHydraulics) the *OpenHydraulics*library and extract it.
4. I n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the File > Import From > Modelica Library .
5. Select the OpenHydraulics folder from your file system.
6. Click Import . 
The *OpenHydraulics* library is imported. The figure below demonstrates the reference between components from *OpenHydraulics* and *Modelica Standard Libraries*: the *RealOuput* type of a Proxy Port *y* which is in *OpenHydraulics* library references to *Modelica Standard Library*. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can create model by dragging Modelica library elements directly on diagram pane. As shown in the figure below, the simple schema is created by dragging *Capacitor* and *Resistor* Blocks on IBD diagram pane and displaying all their ports. To complete schema creation, you need to type names *c* and *r* for a Part Properties and connect ports.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The figure below shows exported model to Modelica filewhich already contains references to the Modelica library components.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For this, do the following:</span></p><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Sharing project data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Share project data >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Sharing+project+data"><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use other projects in a project >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="imported_modelica_library.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Building Modelica libraries in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Using Modelica libraries</h3><p>Let's say we need to use <em>OpenHydraulics</em> modelica library components in a project. Both <em>Modelica Standard Library</em> and <em>OpenHydraulics</em> library<em> </em>must be imported since some components from OpenHydraulics library have references to standard library. </p><p>To use the Modelica libraries in SysML v1 project</p><hr /><ol><li>Create new or open existing SysML v1 project.</li><li>Import the <em>Modelica Standard Library</em>. <ac:link ac:anchor="imp"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Follow the procedure above >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><a href="https://github.com/modelica-3rdparty/OpenHydraulics">D<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">ownload</span></a></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> the <em>OpenHydraulics </em>library and extract it.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">I</span>n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Modelica Library</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li><li>Select the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">OpenHydraulics</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> folder from your file system.</span></li><li>Click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Import</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. <br />The <em>OpenHydraulics</em> library is imported. The figure below demonstrates the reference between components from <em>OpenHydraulics</em> and <em>Modelica Standard Libraries</em>: the <em>RealOuput</em> type of a Proxy Port <em>y</em> which is in <em>OpenHydraulics</em> library references to <em>Modelica Standard Library</em>.</span><br /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="openhydraulicsLib_component_reference_to_modelicaStandardLib_v2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Building Modelica libraries in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can create model by dragging Modelica library elements directly on diagram pane. As shown in the figure below, the simple schema is created by dragging <em>Capacitor</em> and <em>Resistor</em> Blocks on IBD diagram pane and displaying all their ports. To complete schema creation, you need to type names <em>c</em> and <em>r</em>  for a Part Properties and connect ports.</p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_element_from_modelica_library.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Building Modelica libraries in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The figure below shows exported model <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">to Modelica file </span>which already contains references to the Modelica library components.</span></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exported_modelica_lib_model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Building Modelica libraries in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731414 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02455: (2026x) Building NCV-6 matrix

- page_id: `272731414`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731414/2026x+Building+NCV-6+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:53.044+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x) NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (NCV-6) describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities supportdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

The rows of the matrix are Standard Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.

To build a NCV-6 matrix

1. Specify the rows scope (Standard Operational Activities).
2. Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (NCV-6) <span>describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities support </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p>The rows of the matrix are Standard Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NCV-6 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the rows scope (Standard Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p>To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731438 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02456: (2026x) Building NCV-7 matrix

- page_id: `272731438`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731438/2026x+Building+NCV-7+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:56.426+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x) NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Services Mapping Matrix (NCV-7)****depicts which Servicescontribute to the achievement of a Capabilitydirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Capabilities.

To build a NCV-7 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Services Mapping Matrix (NCV-7)<strong> </strong><span>depicts which Services </span><span>contribute to the achievement of a Capability </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p>The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NCV-7 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the Row Scope (Services).</li><li>Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities). </li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh </strong>button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Service </span>to a Capability</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731442 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02457: (2026x) Building NPV-1 matrix

- page_id: `272731442`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731442/2026x+Building+NPV-1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:57.380+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Programme Viewpoint > (2026x) NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an NOV-4 before creating an NPV-1.

The NPV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects. The rows in the NPV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post) and the columns represent Actual Projects.

To build an NPV-1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope ().
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an NOV-4 before creating an NPV-1.</p><p>The NPV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects. The rows in the NPV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post) and the columns represent Actual Projects.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build an NPV-1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> ().</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731567 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02458: (2026x) Building NPV-2 matrix

- page_id: `272731567`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731567/2026x+Building+NPV-2+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:15.914+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Programme Viewpoint > (2026x) NPV-2 Programme to Capability Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**NPV-2 Programme to Capability Mapping** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To build a NPV-2 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Actual Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>NPV-2 Programme to Capability Mapping</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability. </span></p><p>The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NPV-2 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix. </p><p>By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</p><ul><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b597bf3-c2a4-4b05-bcf3-8fb746561ed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Building NPV-2 matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732797 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02459: (2026x) Building NSOV-3 matrix

- page_id: `272732797`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732797/2026x+Building+NSOV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:15.063+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x) NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix purpose is to provide traceability by illustrating which Services support which Operational Activities.

The Rows of this matrix are Services and the Columns are Operational Activities.

To build a NSOV-3 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Services);
2. Specify Column Scope (Operational Activities);
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Operational Activities map to Services using a Supports relationship.

To map Operational Activitiesto Services

Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">purpose is to provide traceability by illustrating which Services support which Operational Activities.</span></p><p>The Rows of this matrix are Services and the Columns are Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NSOV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify<strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services);</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities);</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Building NSOV-3 matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Operational Activities map to Services using a Supports relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Activities </span>to Services</p><hr /><p>Double-click an empty intersection.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><p>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732969 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02460: (2026x) Building NSV-12 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `272732969`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732969/2026x+Building+NSV-12+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:36.272+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-12 Service Provision
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a NSV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.

To build a NSV-12 matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Resource Performers).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.

- Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.
- Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.
- Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The rows in a NSV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NSV-12 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the<strong> Rows scope</strong> (Resource Performers).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a247679b-8dbc-485c-8372-57b68d8fbd26"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732893 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02461: (2026x) Building NSV-3 matrix

- page_id: `272732893`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732893/2026x+Building+NSV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:24.592+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-3 System to System Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems to Systems Matrix (NSV-3) shows how resources interact with each other directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''].NSV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. NSV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To build a NSV-3 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems to Systems Matrix (NSV-3) <span>shows how resources interact with each other directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>NSV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. NSV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NSV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 02462: (2026x) Building NSV-5 matrix

- page_id: `272730157`
- space_key: `MT`

- version_number: 1

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-5 System Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems to Systems Matrix (NSV-3) shows how resources interact with each other directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

The rows of the NSV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To build a NSV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the **Implements** relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

- Click the intersection between the desired elements.

Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

## PAGE 02463: (2026x) Building SOV-3 matrix

- page_id: `272732169`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732169/2026x+Building+SOV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:19.541+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SOV-3 Capability to Service Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Service Mapping View (SOV-3) depicts which Service contribute to the achievement of a Capabilitydirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

To build a SOV-3 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Service Mapping View (SOV-3) <span>depicts which Service contribute to the achievement of a Capability </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SOV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the Row Scope (Services).</li><li>Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities). </li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh </strong>button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Service </span>to a Capability</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731912 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02464: (2026x) Building StV-6 matrix

- page_id: `272731912`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731912/2026x+Building+StV-6+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:49.460+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (StV-6) describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities supportdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

The rows of the matrix are Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.

To build a StV-6 matrix

1. Specify the rows scope (Operational Activities).
2. Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (StV-6) <span>describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities support </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p>The rows of the matrix are Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a StV-6 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the rows scope (Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the Rebuild button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p>To map Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731169 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02465: (2026x) Building SV-12 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `272731169`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731169/2026x+Building+SV-12+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:30.481+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SV-12 Service Provision
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a SV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.

To build a SV-12 matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Resource Performers).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.

- Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.
- Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.
- Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The rows in a SV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SV-12 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the Rows scope (Resource Performers).</li><li>Specify the Columns scope (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5789cb3-91f1-4040-a57c-9fe74ea5b33d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731180 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02466: (2026x) Building SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `272731180`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731180/2026x+Building+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:33.896+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Resource Interaction Matrix (SV-3) shows how resources interact twith each otherdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''].SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

An arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To build a SV-3 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Resource Interaction Matrix (SV-3) <span>shows how resources interact twith each other </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>. </span>SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>An arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the<strong> Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739257 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02467: (2026x) Building SV-5 matrix

- page_id: `272739257`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739257/2026x+Building+SV-5+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:09.395+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5) addresses thedirect and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''])linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.

The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To build a SV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the **Implements** relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

- Click the intersection between the desired elements.

Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5) <span>addresses the </span><span>direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) </span><span>linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.</span></p><p>The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the <strong>Implements</strong> relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c59401e-36e3-4dd4-8e69-d6cfa036ddac"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734931 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02468: (2026x) Building the S4 Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `272734931`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734931/2026x+Building+the+S4+Services+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:13.348+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Service viewpoint > (2026x) S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix describes the directly or using the [implied relations](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Using+Implied+Relations) mapping between the Services and Operational Activities. The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Operational Activities****

To create S4 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Services maps to Operational Activities using the Supports relationship.

To map Services to Operational Activities

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix describes the directly or using the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Using+Implied+Relations">implied relations</a> mapping between the Services and Operational Activities. The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Operational Activities<strong> </strong></p><p><br /></p><p>To create S4 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Services).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Services maps to Operational Activities using the Supports relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map Services to Operational Activities</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736088 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02469: (2026x) Building validation-based metric definitions

- page_id: `272736088`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736088/2026x+Building+validation-based+metric+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:38.378+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools > (2026x) Metric Suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Displaying failing elements

1905924857

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Displaying failing elements

1905924859

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Displaying failing elements

1905924858

A validation-based metric definition is the attribute of a Metric Suite with the «ValidationBasedMetricDefinition» stereotype applied. In this attribute, you can specify a predefined validation rule for calculating metrics according to the parameter values.

800450

To build a validation-based metric definition

1. Select the shape of a Metric Suite, click the smart manipulator on the shape, and select Validation-Based Metric Definition . An attribute with the «ValidationBasedMetricDefinition» stereotype is created. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_validation_based_metric_definition.png']
2. Type the name of the attribute directly on the shape and then click a free space on the diagram.
3. Select the newly created validation-based metric definition on the Metric Suite shape and click [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_type_button.png'] .
4. Select the type of the result values that should be calculated according to the validation rule specified in the validation-based metric definition. Specifying a result typeThis step is mandatory. A validation-based metric definition must have a result type, e.g., Real or Integer.
5. Open the Specification window of the validation-based metric definition.
6. Select the specification cell of the Validation Rule property and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
7. In the **Select Constraint** dialog, select the validation rule that will be used to calculate metrics. Click **OK**.
8. Select the specification cell of the Metric Type property. In the drop down list select the type of the metric you want to calculate.
9. Optionally, select the specification cell of the **Failing Elements Definition** property and enter the name of the menu item that displays failing elements according to the validation rule selected in step 7. Displaying failing elementsTo find the menu item that displays failing elements, right-click the Metric Table cell displaying the metric according to the selected validation rule. By default the menu item is **Display Failing Elements**, unless you give it a different name as described in step 9.

The validation-based metric definition for a Metric Suite is now specified and you can start calculating Metrics by using a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Metric table' space=''].

1905924856

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Metric Suites' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding a new Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specify the target for the Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying parameter definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Building metric definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specific use cases' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
