<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736333 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02577: (2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements

- page_id: `272736333`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736333/2026x+Composite+Structure+diagram+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:03.902+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Importing data from other UML tools and formats > (2026x) Enterprise Architect Import > (2026x) Special Transformation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

559286067

559286070

559286066

**On this page**

3

559286069

This section presents additional EA specific Composite Structure diagram information.

##### Import elements

EA differs from MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM in the Composite Structure diagram's content elements design. The table below shows the differences by focusing on the EA’s elements and how they will be transformed or converted into a format MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM can correctly load and display. The element names shown in the table are the same in both EA and MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM GUI. The contents in the brackets ([…]) are the XMI element type references.

| Enterprise Architect | MagicDraw, CEA, CSM |
| --- | --- |
| Interaction |  |
| Class [uml:Class] | Class [uml:Class] |
| Interface [uml:Interface] | Interface [uml:Interface] |
| Part [uml:Class]Part [uml:Property] | Part [uml:Property] |
| Port [uml:Port] | Port [uml:Port] |
| Collaboration [uml:Collaboration] | Collaboration Use [uml:Collaboration] |
| Expose Interface [not exist] |  |
| Connector [element type is not exist] | Connector [uml:Connector] |
| Assembly [element type is not exist] |  |
| Delegate [element type is not exist] | Connector [uml:Connector] |
| Role Binding [uml:Dependency] | Role Binding [uml:Dependency] |
| Represents [uml:Dependency] | Dependency [uml:Dependency] |
| Occurrence [uml:Dependency] | Dependency [uml:Dependency] |

##### Conversion details

###### Dummy class

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Composite Structure diagram' space=''] needs a Context element to contain itself. However, in EA there is no Context element. After conversion, a dummy [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']element will be created to represent the Context element of Composite Structure diagram.

A dummy Class will be named after the closest owner package of the Composite Structure diagram. The Composite Structure diagram and all of the Composite Structure elements will be placed inside the dummy Class element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dummy class.

###### Class

The Class element data will be normally copied even if the Class element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created to represent the Class element. The Property element will be named after the Class element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Representing a Class Element.

###### Class with Attributes and Operations

The Class view in EA will be converted to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MED']in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space='MED']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MED']will not be shown in the Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part Element after Conversion.

###### Interface

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MED']element data will be normally copied even if the Interface element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created to represent the Interface element. The Property element will be named after the Interface element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Representing Interface Element.

###### Part

There are two types of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MED']:

- Part with a port; and
- Part that sets a type to other elements.

###### **Part with Port**

If you assign the type of a Part element to a Class, Component, or Node, the Part element can have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MED']. If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. Therefore, this Part element can have a Port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that does not Nest Element(s) with Ports.

If a Part is nested to an element and the type is not set, then the Part type will be used to set type to a dummy Class. This particular Part can have a Port. A dummy Class will be created at the same level of the Part element that sets type to it. The dummy Class will be named after the Part concatenating with *_type*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that Nests Element(s) with Post.

###### Part that sets type to other elements

The type of a Part can be set to another element (such as a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Part element and selecting **Advanced**> **Set Property Type**> **Select Property Type.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting Part Type.

If the Part type is set to a type that cannot be the owner of a port, the port will be removed.

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Property element data cannot have any elements. However, in EA, it can have elements. Therefore, if you draw a Part element and it has an element inside it, that particular element will be relocated to an element, which is the Part type.

If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. The element inside the Part will not be relocated. If that particular Part assigns a type to other elements, its type will always be set to Class.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that Sets Type to Other Elements.

If a Nested Part whose type is set to another element (such as a Class, Actor, or Usecase) and this Part contains Nested elements, all of the Nested elements of the Part will be relocated to the element that is the type of this Part.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that Sets Type to Other Elements has Nested Element Inside Itself.

###### portPort

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a Property element that can have a Port is the Property that sets type to a Class, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MED'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='MED'], and other elements cannot have a Port. However, in EA, most of the elements can have a Port.

If a Port is created with an element that is not a Class, Component or Node, the Port will be removed from the diagram view. However, its data will be preserved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Only Class, Component, and Node Can Have a Port.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed view <xmi:id>: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that its type is set to Class, Component and Node only.

###### Port whose type is set to other elements

You can set the type of Port element to another element (a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Port element and selecting **Advanced**> **Set Property Type**> **Select Property Type.**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Setting Port Type.

###### Port whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces

If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the Port type is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element. For example, if the Port1 type is set to Actor, an Interface Realization (the Expose Interface data) will be placed inside the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MED']after conversion.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Port that Sets Type to Other Element Contains Expose Interface.****

###### Collaboration

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space='MED']element data and its nested data will normally be copied. However, if the Collaboration element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram, a Collaboration use element will be created to represent the Collaboration element. The Collaboration use element will be named after the Collaboration element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaboration use.

**Collaboration containing other elements**

A Collaboration can contain only Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction. Elements other than these will be removed. If any element is drawn inside the Collaboration, it will be removed from the diagram view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaboration Containing Other Elements.

The following transformation messages will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine and Interaction only.Removed view <xmi:id>: Invalid Element view. Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.

###### Expose Interface

Expose Interfaces are the Provided and Required interfaces in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. You can draw a Provided Interface or Required Interface in a Port only and set the type of the Port to another element (an Actor, Use Case, or Class). You can create that particular Port on a Part element whose type is set to a Class, Component, or Node only.

###### Expose Interface with Port

If you draw an Expose Interface in a Port and the Port does not assign a type to any elements, that particular Port will be used to set type to a dummy Class. The dummy Class will be created and named after the Port concatenating with *_type*. The Interface element will be moved to the same level of the Port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Expose Interface with Port.

If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the type of the Port is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element (see whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces for more information).

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, some elements can be used as a Port type. These elements can have either a Provided Interface or Required Interface, or both.

The following is a list of the Port's type elements with specific conditions:

- Interfaces can only have a Provided Interface.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Artifact' space='MED'] cannot have a Provided Interface.
- Information Items cannot have a Provided Interface.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Signal Event' space='MED'] cannot have a Provided Interface.
- Components always have both Provided and Required Interfaces.
- The other elements can have both Provided and Required Interfaces.
- After conversion, if the type of a Port is set to an Interface and the Port has an Expose Interface, only the Provided Interface will be shown and the Interface Realization data will be removed.
- Artifacts, Information Items, and Signals cannot hold Interface Realization. If the type of a Port is set to one of them, the Provided Interface will be removed. However, this does not affect the Required Interface.
- If the type of a Port is set to a Component and the Port has an Expose Interface, it will always show both the Provided and Required Interfaces.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Port’s Type Element with Specific Conditions.

The following transformation message will open:

Updated element <xmi:id>: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.

If an Expose Interface is created with an element that is not a Port, the element will be removed both from the Containment tree and diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Expose Interface with Element that is not Port.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.

###### Expose Interface with relationship

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Provided and Required Interfaces cannot be connected with any relationship. If there is any relationship connecting to the Expose Interface, it will be removed from both the Containment tree and diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Expose Interface with Lines.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.

###### Package

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MED']cannot be drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. If it is drawn in EA, it will be removed from the diagram once it has been converted to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Package.

###### Assembly

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, there is no Assembly line. It will be updated to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED'].

The following transformation message will open:

Updated element <xmi:id>: Assembly updated to Connector.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Updating the Assembly Line to the Connector Line.

###### Dependency

There are many relationships that EA exporter exports to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MED'] as the following elements:

- Delegate
- Role Binding
- Represents
- Occurrence
- Nest
- Derive
- Import
- Instantiate
- Usage
- Realize
- Trace

The above relationships will be shown in the diagram view as Dependencies with a stereotype.

###### Removed relationships

If an Association, Direct Association, Aggregation, Composition, Generalization, or Specialization is created in a Composite Structure diagram, it will be removed from the diagram view, but its data will be preserved.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Removing lines.

The following transformation messages will open:

Removed view <xmi:id>: Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.

##### Transformation report

A report containing the conflicts solved during transformation, along with other transformation information (such as special mapping and removal of some irrelevant data), is required to be provided to the users.

The following is a list of the transformation messages:

Removed view <xmi:id>: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that set type to Class, Component, and Node only.Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction only.Removed view <xmi:id>: Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.Removed view <xmi:id>: UseCase cannot be contained in any element view.Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.Updated element <xmi:id>: Assembly updated to Connector.Updated element <xmi:id>: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.Removed view <xmi:id>: Association cannot be shown in CompositeStructure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.

559293207

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Composite Structure diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="158f7b73-1490-48a9-a999-4df2fe58b187"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286067</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62ac6b24-c1ed-4e94-9c0d-fe56da72572b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286070</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e56e68c8-3d58-444e-98f7-591b87e1679c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286066</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4913ad71-84d1-4193-be83-4f29b776f185"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9578db90-5035-4e33-a77a-fdc90bb7e706"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286069</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><br /></p><p>This section presents additional EA specific Composite Structure diagram information.</p><h3>Import elements</h3><p>EA differs from MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM in the Composite Structure diagram's content elements design. The table below shows the differences by focusing on the EA’s elements and how they will be transformed or converted into a format MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM can correctly load and display. The element names shown in the table are the same in both EA and MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM GUI. The contents in the brackets ([…]) are the XMI element type references.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Enterprise Architect</th><th>MagicDraw, CEA, CSM</th></tr><tr><td>Interaction</td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td><span>Class [uml:Class]</span></td><td><span>Class [uml:Class] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Interface [uml:Interface]</span></td><td><p>Interface [uml:Interface]</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Part [uml:Class]</p><p>Part [uml:Property]</p></td><td><p>Part [uml:Property]</p></td></tr><tr><td><span>Port<span> </span>[uml:Port]</span></td><td><span>Port<span> </span>[uml:Port]</span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Collaboration [uml:Collaboration]</span></td><td><span>Collaboration Use [uml:Collaboration] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Expose Interface [not<span> </span>exist]</span></td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td><span>Connector [element type is not exist]</span></td><td><span>Connector [uml:Connector] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Assembly [element type is not exist]</span></td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td><span>Delegate [element type is not exist]</span></td><td><span>Connector [uml:Connector]</span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Role Binding [uml:Dependency]</span></td><td><span>Role Binding [uml:Dependency] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Represents [uml:Dependency]</span></td><td><span>Dependency [uml:Dependency] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Occurrence [uml:Dependency]            <span> </span></span></td><td><span>Dependency [uml:Dependency]</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Conversion details</h3><h4>Dummy class</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> needs a Context element to contain itself. However, in EA there is no Context element. After conversion, a dummy <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element will be created to represent the Context element of Composite Structure diagram.</p><p>A dummy Class will be named after the closest owner package of the Composite Structure diagram. The Composite Structure diagram and all of the Composite Structure elements will be placed inside the dummy Class element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dummyClass.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dummy class.</h6><h4>Class</h4><p>The Class element data will be normally copied even if the Class element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created  to represent the Class element. The Property element will be named after the Class element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="propertyrepresent_class.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Representing a Class Element.</h6><h5>Class with Attributes and Operations</h5><p>The Class view in EA will be converted to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Attribute" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Attributes ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operations ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>will not be shown in the Composite Structure diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="partElement_converted.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part Element after Conversion.</h6><h4>Interface</h4><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interface ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element data will be normally copied even if the Interface element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created to represent the Interface element. The Property element will be named after the Interface element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="propertyrepresent_interface.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Representing Interface Element.</h6><h4>Part</h4><p>There are two types of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:</p><ul><li>Part with a port; and </li><li>Part that sets a type to other elements.</li></ul><h5><strong>Part with Port</strong></h5><p>If you assign the type of a Part element to a Class, Component, or Node, the Part element can have a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Port ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. Therefore, this Part element can have a Port.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_withoutNestElement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that does not Nest Element(s) with Ports.</h6><p>If a Part is nested to an element and the type is not set, then the Part type will be used to set type to a dummy Class. This particular Part can have a Port. A dummy Class will be created at the same level of the Part element that sets type to it. The dummy Class will be named after the Part concatenating with <em>_type</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_withNestElement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that Nests Element(s) with Post.</h6><h5>Part that sets type to other elements</h5><p>The type of a Part can be set to another element (such as  a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Part element and selecting  <strong>Advanced </strong>&gt; <strong>Set Property Type </strong>&gt; <strong>Select Property Type.</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="partType.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Setting Part Type.</h6><p>If the Part type is set to a type that cannot be the owner of a port, the port will be removed.</p><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Property element data cannot have any elements. However, in EA, it can have elements. Therefore, if you draw a Part element and it has an element inside it, that particular element will be relocated to an element, which is the Part type.</p><p>If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. The element inside the Part will not be relocated. If that particular Part assigns a type to other elements, its type will always be set to Class.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_setType_otherElement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that Sets Type to Other Elements.</h6><p>If a Nested Part whose type is set to another element (such as a Class, Actor, or Usecase) and this Part contains Nested elements, all of the Nested elements of the Part will be relocated to the element that is the type of this Part.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_setType_otherElementwithNest.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that Sets Type to Other Elements has Nested Element Inside Itself.</h6><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfb33356-5197-4c86-acec-96a4507efd85"><ac:parameter ac:name="">port</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Port</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a Property element that can have a Port is the Property that sets type to a Class, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Component" /></ac:link>, or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Node" /></ac:link>, and other elements cannot have a Port. However, in EA, most of the elements can have a Port.</p><p>If a Port is created with an element that is not a Class, Component or Node, the Port will be removed from the diagram view. However, its data will be preserved.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_component_nodewithport.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Only Class, Component, and Node Can Have a Port.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that its type is set to Class, Component and Node only.</pre></blockquote><h5>Port whose type is set to other elements</h5><p>You can set the type of Port element to another element (a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Port element and selecting <strong>Advanced </strong>&gt; <strong>Set Property Type </strong>&gt; <strong>Select Property Type.</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="portType.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Setting Port Type.</h6><h5>Port whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces</h5><p>If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the Port type is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element. For example, if the Port1 type is set to Actor, an Interface Realization (the Expose Interface data) will be placed inside the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actor ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>after conversion.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_setTypetoElement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Port that Sets Type to Other Element Contains Expose Interface.<strong><br /></strong></h6><h4>Collaboration</h4><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Collaboration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Collaboration ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element data and its nested data will normally be copied. However, if the Collaboration element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram, a Collaboration use element will be created to represent the Collaboration element. The Collaboration use element will be named after the Collaboration element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaborationUse.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Collaboration use.</h6><p><strong>Collaboration containing other elements</strong></p><p>A Collaboration can contain only Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction. Elements other than these will be removed. If any element is drawn inside the Collaboration, it will be removed from the diagram view.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_containElement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Collaboration Containing Other Elements.</h6><p>The following transformation messages will open:</p><blockquote><ul><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine and Interaction only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element view. Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.</pre></li></ul></blockquote><h4>Expose Interface</h4><p>Expose Interfaces are the Provided and Required interfaces in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. You can draw a Provided Interface or Required Interface in a Port only and set the type of the Port to another element (an Actor, Use Case, or Class). You can create that particular Port on a Part element whose type is set to a Class, Component, or Node only.</p><h5>Expose Interface with Port</h5><p>If you draw an Expose Interface in a Port and the Port does not assign a type to any elements, that particular Port will be used to set type to a dummy Class. The dummy Class will be created and named after the Port concatenating with <em>_type</em>. The Interface element will be moved to the same level of the Port.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exposeInterface_withPort.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: right;">Expose Interface with Port.</h6><p>If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the type of the Port is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element (see <ac:link ac:anchor="port"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Port ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces for more information).</p><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, some elements can be used as a Port type. These elements can have either a Provided Interface or Required Interface, or both.</p><p>The following is a list of the Port's type elements with specific conditions:</p><ul><li>Interfaces can only have a Provided Interface.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Artifact" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Artifacts ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>cannot have a Provided Interface.</li><li>Information Items cannot have a Provided Interface.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Signal Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Signals ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>cannot have a Provided Interface.</li><li>Components always have both Provided and Required Interfaces.</li><li>The other elements can have both Provided and Required Interfaces.</li><li>After conversion, if the type of a Port is set to an Interface and the Port has an Expose Interface, only the Provided Interface will be shown and the Interface Realization data will be removed.</li><li>Artifacts, Information Items, and Signals cannot hold Interface Realization. If the type of a Port is set to one of them, the Provided Interface will be removed. However, this does not affect the Required Interface.</li><li>If the type of a Port is set to a Component and the Port has an Expose Interface, it will always show both the Provided and Required Interfaces.</li></ul><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_withTypeElement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Port’s Type Element with Specific Conditions.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.</pre></blockquote><p>If an Expose Interface is created with an element that is not a Port, the element will be removed both from the Containment tree and diagram pane.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exposeInterface_withElement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Expose Interface with Element that is not Port.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.</pre></blockquote><h5>Expose Interface with relationship</h5><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Provided and Required Interfaces cannot be connected with any relationship. If there is any relationship connecting to the Expose Interface, it will be removed from both the Containment tree and diagram pane.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exposeInterface_withLines.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Expose Interface with Lines.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.</pre></blockquote><h4>Package</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>cannot be drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. If it is drawn in EA, it will be removed from the diagram once it has been converted to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="package.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Package.</h6><h4>Assembly</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, there is no Assembly line. It will be updated to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Connector" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Assembly updated to Connector.</pre></blockquote><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="assemblyLine_toConnectorLine.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Updating the Assembly Line to the Connector Line.</h6><h4>Dependency</h4><p>There are many relationships that EA exporter exports to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Dependency" /></ac:link> as the following elements:</p><ul><li>Delegate</li><li>Role Binding</li><li>Represents</li><li>Occurrence</li><li>Nest</li><li>Derive</li><li>Import</li><li>Instantiate</li><li>Usage</li><li>Realize</li><li>Trace</li></ul><p>The above relationships will be shown in the diagram view as Dependencies with a stereotype.</p><h4>Removed relationships</h4><p>If an Association, Direct Association, Aggregation, Composition, Generalization, or Specialization is created in a Composite Structure diagram, it will be removed from the diagram view, but its data will be preserved.</p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="removeLines.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Removing lines.</h6><p>The following transformation messages will open:</p><blockquote><ul><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li></ul></blockquote><h3>Transformation report</h3><p>A report containing the conflicts solved during transformation, along with other transformation information (such as special mapping and removal of some irrelevant data), is required to be provided to the users.</p><p>The following is a list of the transformation messages:</p><blockquote><ul><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that set type to Class, Component, and Node only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: UseCase cannot be contained in any element view.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.</pre></li><li><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Assembly updated to Connector.</pre></li><li><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Association cannot be shown in CompositeStructure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li></ul></blockquote></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f3bce0e-d6b4-4f89-8bf3-c27fcd3116b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559293207</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739056 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02578: (2026x) Composition

- page_id: `272739056`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739056/2026x+Composition
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:46:37.483+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x) Organization Structure Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1488114690

1488114692

1488114692

**Description**

A Composition is a key relationship that shows how organization structure is composed. For example, one organization unit can be a part of a parent organization.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1488114689

**Related elements**

- Resource
- Organization Unit
- Role
- Person
- Information system

**Related diagrams**

- Organization Structure Diagram

**Related Procedure**

- Using Organization Structure Diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5b1af80-e418-4d62-806b-1ce284f916ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1488114690</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d454e12-c3f6-41ca-8f2d-9d446f555f3b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1488114692</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6174ea0-d8ab-4fab-b483-823aeb5f6849"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1488114692</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Composition is a key relationship that shows how organization structure is composed. For example, one organization unit can be a part of a parent organization.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="composition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composition" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="455d27a2-740a-47e1-8753-7cc1a3eaf3fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1488114689</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Resource">Resource</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Organization+Unit">Organization Unit</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Role">Role</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Person">Person</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Information+system">Information system</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Organization+Structure+Diagram">Organization Structure Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related Procedure</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Using+Organization+Structure+Diagram">Using Organization Structure Diagram</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730080 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02579: (2026x) Compositors

- page_id: `272730080`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730080/2026x+Compositors
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:31.771+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Data-related modeling > (2026x) XML schemas > (2026x) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Complex type maps to UML Class with the stereotype XSDcomplexType. In order to have a group in a complex type, the same UML Class must also have the XSDall, XSDchoice or XSDsequence stereotype.

A UML model can have a ModelClass with only a single stereotype XSDall, XSDchoice, or XSDsequence. In this case, the class maps to the inner part of another group.

Element order in a sequence group is very important. These elements are ordered according to the values of TaggedValue sequenceOrder.

#### PANEL: compositors XML representation summary

compositors XML representation summary

```text
<all
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
maxOccurs = 1 : 1
```

```text
minOccurs = (0 | 1) : 1
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace...}>	Content: (annotation?, element*)</all>
```

```text
<choice
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
maxOccurs = (nonNegativeInteger | unbounded):1
```

```text
minOccurs = nonNegativeInteger : 1 	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?, (element | group | choice | sequence | any)*)
```

```text
</choice>
```

```text
<sequence
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
maxOccurs = (nonNegativeInteger | unbounded) : 1
```

```text
minOccurs = nonNegativeInteger : 1
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?, (element | group | choice | sequence | any)*)</sequence>
```

```

```text

```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737796 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02580: (2026x) Concepts

- page_id: `272737796`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737796/2026x+Concepts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:18.109+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard includes the following concepts:

- Template specifies the document layout, format, corresponding model elements, and custom defined fields.
- Report Data specifies a set of custom fields and data in the selected template. A template can have a number of different custom defined fields for a project or different projects organized in a report.
- Report a generated user document with data from the project displayed in the selected template style.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard includes the following concepts:</p><ul><li><strong>Template<br /></strong>specifies the document layout, format, corresponding model elements, and custom defined fields.</li><li><strong>Report Data</strong> <br />specifies a set of custom fields and data in the selected template. A template can have a number of different custom <br />defined fields for a project or different projects organized in a report.</li><li><strong>Report</strong> <br />a generated user document with data from the project displayed in the selected template style.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730245 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02581: (2026x) Concepts viewpoint

- page_id: `272730245`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730245/2026x+Concepts+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:43.920+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0
- labels: ['concepts-viewpoint', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

819027803

819027805

819027804

The Concepts viewpoint supports the process of analyzing and optimizing the delivery of capabilities in line with NATO‘s strategic intent. It achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision, concepts, NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The Concepts viewpoint further details the dependencies between capabilities, enabling capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.

819027802

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Concepts viewpoint</span> further details the dependencies between capabilities, enabling capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="98521962-e1c0-4fcc-9cb3-d8343aa27cde"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">819027802</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Views</span></strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f2a62c08-3be3-4c85-9feb-417da880ca6c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738385 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02582: (2026x) Conditional Boundary Event

- page_id: `272738385`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738385/2026x+Conditional+Boundary+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:45:06.068+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x) Boundary Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Conditional Boundary Event is triggered when a specified condition becomes true. When the event is triggered, it will change a normal flow of a Process into an exception flow.

This event displays either of the following on a diagram:

- A Conditional Catching Intermediate Event name if the name is specified.
- A Conditional property value if the name is unspecified.

**Notation**

- Conditional Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - True) [ATTACHMENT filename='boundary_event_conditional.png']
- Non-interrupting Conditional Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - True) [ATTACHMENT filename='boundary_event_conditional_false.png']

**Related elements**

- Boundary Events
- Activities

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737867 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02584: (2026x) Conditional Start Event

- page_id: `272737867`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737867/2026x+Conditional+Start+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:44:35.714+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x) Start Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Conditional Start Event is triggered when a condition is specified, become true. For example, “S&P 500 changes by more than 10% since opening” or “Temperature above 300C”.

A Conditional Start Event displays either of the following on a diagram:

- A Conditional Start Event name if the name is specified.
- A Condition property value if the name is unspecified.

A conditional expression of an event must become “false”, and then “true” before the event can be triggered again.

**Notation**

- Interrupting Conditional Start Event [ATTACHMENT filename='start_event_conditional_interr.png']
- Non-interrupting Conditional Start Event [ATTACHMENT filename='start_event_conditional_noninterr.png']

**Related element**

- Start Events

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739811 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02585: (2026x) Configuration files

- page_id: `272739811`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739811/2026x+Configuration+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:00.070+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A configuration file is where the initial parameters and settings of a program are defined. All default parameter values come with a program installation but you can edit configuration files and define your own values, for example, define the Java home directory. This page describes the locations where you can find the configuration files of your modeling tool, and how you can modify them and change their default storage location. Configuration files can be used to:

- Define application arguments
- Set the Java Home
- Change Java parameters (such as memory allocation)
- Update default Java classpath (advanced: for plugin developers)

The configuration files can be opened and modified using any standard text or code editor. Once modified, changing the default location of configuration files can allow you to store multiple configuration files for different versions of the tool.

This page covers all modeling tools:

- MagicDraw
- Cameo Enterprise Architecture
- Cameo Systems Modeler
- Magic Software Architect
- Magic Cyber Systems Engineer
- Magic Systems of Systems Architect

By default the configuration and auxiliary files of a modeling tool are stored in the following location:

| OS | Configuration files storage |
| --- | --- |
| WindowsVista/7/8/10 | C:\Users\<USERNAME>\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version number> |
| Other OS | <user.home>/.<modeling tool name>/< modeling tool version number> |

#### INFO: Modeling tool file naming

Modeling tool file naming

In a file system, modeling tools' directorys are named as follows:

| Modeling tool | File name |
| --- | --- |
| MagicDraw | magicdraw |
| Cameo Enterprise Architecture | cameoea |
| Cameo Systems Modeler | cameo.systems.modeler |
| Magic Software Architect | magic.software.architect |
| Magic Cyber Systems Engineer | magic.cyber.systems.engineer |
| Magic Systems of Systems Architect | magic.systems.of.systems.architect |

##### Getting the path to configuration files

If you do not know the exact path to your modeling tool configuration files, follow the steps below to get it.

To get the path to the configuration files

1. From the Help menu, select About <modeling tool name>. The About dialog opens.
2. Click the Environment tab.
3. Click the hyperlink next to Configuration Files . The directory containing modeling tool configuration files opens.

You can also save configuration files in any of the following locations:

- <modeling tool installation directory>
- Common Application Data location (on Windows OS)
- Another directory

##### Storing configuration files in the installation directory or Common Application Data location on Windows

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The Common Application Data location on Windows Vista/7/8/10 is *C:\ProgramData*.

To store configuration files in the modeling tool installation directory or Common Application Data location on Windows

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located in <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, change the -DLOCALCONFIG property value according to the following table:

| -DLOCALCONFIG | =true | =false |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Windows OSx | Common Application Data | Modeling tool installation directory |
| Other | <user.home>/.<modeling tool name>/< modeling tool version number> | Modeling tool installation directory |

#### WARNING: Important!

Important!

It is important to add the **-DWINCONFIG=false** parameter on Window OS, otherwise, the configuration files will not be stored in the modeling tool installation directory.

You may also store configuration files in your custom location.

##### Storing configuration files in a custom location

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you have defined to store files in the modeling tool installation directory, files will not be stored in the defined location.

To store modeling tool configuration files in a custom location

Do either:

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located in <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property: -Dlocalconfig.location=<absolute path to a custom location> Sample property value-Dlocalconfig.location\=E\:\\temp\\innerPack

Or:

1. Go to the directory, where .
2. Create a new file named *redirected.location*.
3. In the file, type the absolute path to the directory, wherein modeling configuration files will be saved, e.g., C:/<directory name>.

The path in both options (*localconfig.location* property/*redirected.location* file) can contain system/environment/application related variables.

| Context of the variable | Format of the variable | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Java system property | <sys:variableName> | <sys:user.name> |
| OS environment variable | <env:variableName> | <env:USERNAME> |
| Application related variable | <app:variableName> | <app:version> |

There are only two application related variables:

| Application related variable | Description | Value example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| version | Full version of the application | 2024x Refresh3 |
| default.config.dir.name | Default modeling tool configuration directory name (.<modeling tool name>) | .magicdraw (see ) |

#### TIP: Sample path with variables

Sample path with variables

```text
<env:APPDATA>/configurations/<app:default.config.dir.name>/<sys:user.name>/<app:version>
```

##### Installing JxBrowser extensions

You caninstall custom browser extensions in JxBrowser as described below.

To install JxBrowser extensions

1. Create a new data/jxbrowser/extensions directory.
2. Add the JxBrowser extension files (.crx) you want to install to the data/jxbrowser/extensions directory.
3. Start or restart your modeling tool.

After completing the above steps, the JxBrowser extensions added to the *extensions* directory will be installed in all JxBrowser instances.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing environment options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing and Selecting Perspective' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737567 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02586: (2026x) Configuring report engine properties

- page_id: `272737567`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737567/2026x+Configuring+report+engine+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:44.774+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Configuring Report Wizard > (2026x) Report Wizard Environment options
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can configure the report properties of Report Wizard in the **Environment Options** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Configure report properties pane.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can select a report property in the **Configure report properties** pane, and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to see its description.

To edit, add, or delete a report property

1. Click **Options** > **Environment** to open the **Environment Options** dialog.
2. Select **Report Wizard** from the tree view to open the **Configure report properties** pane.
3. Click: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to edit a selected property name and value in the **Report Properties**dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a property name and value in the **Report Properties** dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to delete a selected property name and value from the **Configure report properties** pane.

The following table lists the fields of the **Report Properties**dialog:

| Field Name | Function | Default Value | Possible Value | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Name | The property name | Blank | Text | Yes |
| Value | The property value | Blank | Text | No |
| Description | The property description |  | Text | No |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737551 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02588: (2026x) Configuring template mappings

- page_id: `272737551`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737551/2026x+Configuring+template+mappings
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:43.870+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Configuring Report Wizard > (2026x) Report Wizard Environment options
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the **Environment Options** dialog to configure the template mappings of Report Wizard.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Configure template mappings pane.

To edit, create, or delete a template mapping

1. Click **Options** > **Environment**. The **Environment Options** dialog will open.
2. Select **Report Wizard** from the tree view. The **Configure template mappings** pane will open.
3. Click: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to edit a selected filename extension and template type in the **T****emplate mappings**dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a filename extension and template type in the **T****emplate mappings** dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to delete a selected filename extension and template type from the **Configure template mappings**pane.

The following table lists the fields of the **T****emplate mappings** dialog:

| Field Name | Function | Default Value | Possible Value | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| File extension | The filename extension (not case-sensitive). | Blank | Text | Yes |
| Template type | The template type. You can select any of the following: html, odp, docbook, dbk, odt, ods, docx. | Blank | Text | Yes |

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

Deleting a template mapping from the**Environment Options**dialog will cause the file extension mapping to be removed from the modeling tool. To restore the default setting, restart the modeling tool.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog will open.</p></li><li><p>Select <strong>Report Wizard</strong> from the tree view. The <strong>Configure template mappings</strong> pane will open.</p></li><li><p>Click:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="EditProperties_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Configuring template mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to edit a selected filename extension and template type in the <strong>T</strong><strong>emplate mappings </strong>dialog, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Configuring template mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="AddProperties_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Configuring template mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to create a filename extension and template type in the <strong>T</strong><strong>emplate mappings</strong> dialog, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Configuring template mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="RemoveProperties_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Configuring template mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to delete a selected filename extension and template type from the <strong>Configure template mappings </strong>pane.</p><p><br /></p></li></ol><p>The following table lists the fields of the <strong>T</strong><strong>emplate mappings</strong> dialog:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Field Name </th><th>Function </th><th>Default Value </th><th>Possible Value </th><th>Required</th></tr><tr><td>File extension</td><td>The filename extension (not case-sensitive).</td><td>Blank</td><td>Text </td><td>Yes </td></tr><tr><td>Template type </td><td><p>The template type. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731190 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02589: (2026x) Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram

- page_id: `272731190`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731190/2026x+Connecting+Blocks+in+SysML+Internal+Block+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:38.089+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the composite [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']are created in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''], they can be instantiated on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED']. These Part Properties are assembled through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED'], linking them directly or via their ports.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']An internal structure of *Coffee Machine* Block where Part Properties are connected via Proxy Ports by using the Connector.

Read the following sections to learn how to connect Part Properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating a relationship' space='']
- Connecting parts through interface
- Creating Item Flow
- Implied Connectors
- Enforce Ports Compatibility mode
- [Using Association Block as Connector type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Using+Association+Block+as+Connector+type)

- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\Simulation\CoffeeMachine.mdzip.

**Sample model**

The model used in the figure of this page is the **Coffee Machine** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='CoffeeMachine.mdzip'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>After the composite <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Blocks ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>are created in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Block Definition Diagram" /></ac:link>, they can be instantiated on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link> as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. These Part Properties are assembled through the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connectors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, linking them directly or via their ports.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ibd_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>An internal structure of <em>Coffee Machine</em> Block where Part Properties are connected via Proxy Ports by using the <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731209 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02590: (2026x) Connecting parts through interface

- page_id: `272731209`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731209/2026x+Connecting+parts+through+interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:37.447+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can drag the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED'] directly on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED'] relationship to:

- Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] at both ends of the Connector.
- Set types (interfaces) of Proxy Ports.
- Set directions of Proxy Ports.
- Keep layout of the Connector .

That helps to ensure an efficient interface modeling in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].

To connect [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] through interface

1. Create a Connector between two [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] .
2. Drag an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED'] on the Connector.
3. From the opened menu, select Connect Through Interface command. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] with directions and interfaces are created. [ATTACHMENT filename='dnd_on_connector.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can drag the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interface Block" /></ac:link> directly on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Connector" /></ac:link> relationship to:</p><ul><li>Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Proxy Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Proxy Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> at both ends of the Connector.</li><li>Set types (interfaces) of Proxy Ports.</li><li>Set directions of Proxy Ports.</li><li>Keep <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">layout of the Connector</span></span>.</li></ul><p>That helps to ensure an efficient interface modeling in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Internal Block Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> through interface</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Connector between two <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Drag an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interface Block" /></ac:link> on the Connector.</li><li>From the opened menu, select <strong>Connect Through Interface</strong> command.<br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Proxy Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Proxy Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with directions and interfaces are created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dnd_on_connector.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Connecting parts through interface" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736170 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02591: (2026x) Connectors

- page_id: `272736170`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736170/2026x+Connectors
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:49.605+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Sample models > (2026x) Creating a simple model > (2026x) Blocks and Parts
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the Circuit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''], it is now possible to display the ports on the parts and connect them using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding Connector' space='MED'] (see the figure below).

Ports can be displayed in IBD by selecting all existing parts, then right-clicking and from the shortcut menuselecting the **Related Elements** >**Display Ports**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Three parts are connected trough the *p and n* ports.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the Circuit <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Internal Block Diagram (IBD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, it is now possible to display the ports on the parts and connect them using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Binding Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connectors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (see the figure below).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06ffada6-f2b1-4511-a1d2-2a132a6beda5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Ports can be displayed in IBD by selecting all existing parts, then right-clicking and <span>from the shortcut menu </span>selecting the <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt;<strong> Display Ports</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="internal_structure_of_circuit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Connectors" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Three parts are connected trough the <em>p and n</em> ports.</h6><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738153 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02592: (2026x) Consecutive with Owner Number

- page_id: `272738153`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738153/2026x+Consecutive+with+Owner+Number
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:45:52.562+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) Numbering Elements > (2026x) Numbering Schemas
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486805673

1486805674

1486805674

**Description**

A Consecutive with Owner Number numbering schema provides non-multilevel element numbering. An Element owner (BPMN Process, BPMN Collaboration, or Choreography) number is displayed before the element number.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Consecutive Numbering style with element owner number

1486805672

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Using BPMN Element Numbers

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

An Element owner (BPMN Process, BPMN Collaboration, or Choreography) number is displayed before the element number.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_numbering_consecutive_I.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Consecutive with Owner Number" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Consecutive Numbering style with element owner number</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ad2901e-672b-4fda-a0c9-37e4152e8287"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486805672</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Using+BPMN+Element+Numbers">Using BPMN Element Numbers</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 02593: (2026x) Consecutive without Owner Number

- page_id: `272738147`

1486832938

1486832938

**Description**

A Consecutive without Owner Number numbering schema provides non-multilevel element numbering. An Element Owner (BPMN Process, BPMN Collaboration, or Choreography) number is not included in the element number.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Consecutive numbering style without owner number

1486832937

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Using BPMN Element Numbers

## PAGE 02594: (2026x) Constraint properties and bindings

- page_id: `272736232`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736232/2026x+Constraint+properties+and+bindings
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:54.803+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Sample models > (2026x) Creating a simple model > (2026x) Component Behavior
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The previously created constraints need to be bound to the variable of each block. To do so, constraint properties are created in each block, and the block variables and parameters are bound to the constraint parameters accordingly. Images bellow show the bindings for the ground, the source, and the resistor respectively.

To create bindings, it is necessary to display the ports as well as their parts using the shortcut menu (**Related Elements** > **Display Ports**and **Related Elements** >**Display Parts**).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The SysML Parametric Diagram of *Ground* Block.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The SysML Parametric Diagram of *Source*Block.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The SysML Parametric Diagram of *Resistor*Block.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
